Title: Business Data Analyst
HTML:
Company Description

Inetum is a European leader in digital services. Inetum’s team of 27,000 consultants and specialists strive every day to make a digital impact for businesses, public sector entities and society. Inetum’s solutions aim at contributing to its clients’ performance and innovation as well as the common good. Present in 19 countries with a dense network of sites, Inetum partners with major software publishers to meet the challenges of digital transformation with proximity and flexibility. Driven by its ambition for growth and scale, Inetum generated sales of 2.4 billion euros in 2024.

Job Description

Are you passionate about turning data into actionable insights? At Inetum, we are looking for a Business Data Analyst to bridge the gap between data and business, ensuring that information is available to support strategic decisions efficiently.

Key Responsibilities

  • Gather and define business requirements for data initiatives;
  • Extract and analyze data from commercial and analytical systems for ad hoc analysis;
  • Design and structure data models to create valuable products and ensure data consistency;
  • Perform business analysis to support operations and decision-making;
  • Communicate effectively with stakeholders and translate insights into actionable recommendations;
  • Contribute to the evolution of our Data Warehouse and Datahub.

Deliverables

  • Development of dashboards and data visualization strategies;
  • Creation of documents and reports to support decision-making;
  • Compilation of information and preparation of analytical reports;
  • User Stories for Data Warehouse enhancements;
  • Data modeling within the Data Warehouse;
  • Ad hoc data collection from commercial and analytical systems.

Qualifications

  • Up to 10 years of experience in similar roles;
  • Advanced knowledge of SQL for data extraction and manipulation;
  • Strong experience with dashboards and data visualization tools;
  • Advanced Excel and proficiency in Office tools;
  • Preferably experience with SAS Enterprise Guide and DOMO;
  • Solid understanding of data modeling and warehousing concepts.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About The Job


People Analytics is the data expertise center of the HR department. Its goal is to support HR teams in leveraging data to help strategically manage activities and improve employee experience. To achieve this, People Analytics relies on a data hub that gathers HR data from various sources as well as on data processing, data visualization, and data science tools. People Analytics offers analyses, dashboards, predictive models, and digital solutions that meet the needs of senior management, HR teams, and business units. The team is particularly responsible for monitoring and analyzing workforce trends, and sharing these figures with our external stakeholders and senior management. Within People Analytics, you will join a dynamic and multidisciplinary team of about forty collaborators, working in an agile mode (data analysts, data engineers, data scientists) and will be in constant contact with other HR department collaborators. As a Data Analyst, in collaboration with the team’s data scientists and data engineers, you will build analyses and participate in the development of projects that help make strategic decisions for the function.


Your Main Activities Are


Understand the needs expressed by senior management, HR teams, or the business
Translate them into an analytical roadmap (scope of analysis, metrics...) to help make decisions
Develop dashboards (Power BI and Tableau) and analyses independently (preparation, exploration, descriptive analysis, data visualization...) or in collaboration with data scientists (clustering, machine learning, NLP...) and data engineers of the team (industrialization phase)
Contribute to the monitoring and analysis of workforce trends, from data collection to the delivery of analyses to senior management
Clearly communicate actionable recommendations orally and in writing through reliable and quality deliverables


Profile And Skills To Success


Academic Background in Finance, Management or Audit
Fluency in English
Quantitative and analytical skills
Organizational skills
Communication skills (oral and written)
Data Quality and Security
Problem-solving Skills
Knowledge in Tableau; PowerBI, Excel and DataIKU


Why joining BNP Paribas?


Leading banking institution


Leading player in banking and financial services in Europe, BNP Paribas operates in 64 countries and has nearly 178,000 employees, including more than 144,000 in Europe


International reach


The Group holds key positions in several areas of banking and financial services that are structured around three operating divisions:


Corporate & Institutional Banking (CIB) connects companies needing financing and institutional clients looking for investment opportunities division that offers tailored financial solutions for corporate and institutional clients.
Commercial, Personal Banking & Services (CPBS) brings together all our commercial banks and several specialised businesses
Investment & Protection Services (IPS) combines a unique continuum of products and services across protection, saving, investment and real estate including specialised businesses offering a wide range of savings, investment and protection services
Our presence in Portugal


In Portugal since 1985, BNP Paribas today has more than 9.220 employees, distributed across the Group's 10 business entities established in the country. Its presence also extends to 11 centres of expertise providing value-added services to various countries where the BNP Paribas Group also operates.


Diversity and Inclusion commitment


BNP Paribas ´ambition Is To Cultivate An Open And Responsive Environment For All That Encourages Collaboration And Interaction. We Demonstrate Our Commitment By Actively Contributing To The Group’s Diversity And Inclusion Strategy And Goals, In Line With The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. As Part Of Our Local Corporate And Social Responsibility – Diversity & Inclusion Strategy, BNP Paribas Portugal Is Committed To


Promote a fair and unbiased recruitment process and offer professional development opportunities to all employees
Celebrate diversity and advocate for inclusion, both externally and internally, encouraging employee’s participation, creating space for different voices to be heard.


To foster the effort of BNP Paribas Portugal, multiple initiatives and events take place throughout the year where our people can find out more on the importance of diversity inclusion at the workplace and in our society.


Remote Working Conditions


At BNP Paribas, we embrace a Smart Working framework based on trust, autonomy and collaboration. Within this framework, eligible employees can benefit from flexible remote working options adapted to our hybrid working environment. To ensure a comfortable and efficient working setup, eligible employees are provided with both office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional items at reduced prices.


Commitment towards work/life balance


Supporting employees in a hybrid way of working while providing them with the means to maintain a work-life balance is an essential dimension of our Smart Working program. At BNP Paribas, we care about our employees’ wellbeing and promote a culture of good integration between work and personal life.

To find out more on why you should join BNP Paribas please read our Employee Value Proposition and our Career path page.


Please note that only applications submitted in English will be considered.
In case you are selected for this role, further documentation will be requested to support your hiring process.

Title: Data Analyst Trainee
Description:
About The Job


The Transaction Banking business concerns two fundamental business lines:
Cash Management: all products that assist our customers with their liquidity needs (current accounts, term deposits, payments & collections, forex)
Global Trade Solutions: all products that assist our customers with their global trade needs (letters of credit, guarantees, supply chain management, factoring)
As Transaction Banking Chief Operating Officer (TB COO) team, we assist our different business team in multiple topics, both on daily/monthly needs as in project mode. Within the COO team we have a small team of data scientists focusing on data, who are involved in the data use cases from exploration phase up to production delivery.
This position provides an excellent opportunity to learn about the overall banking industry as a whole, more specifically dealing with the Transaction Banking business across all regions (EMEA, APAC, AMER)
The Data Analyst Trainee’s main mission is to provide support to the Cash Management fee analysis Unit and to contribute to the pricing to billing initiatives

Your Main Activities Are


Automate business dashboards as prototypes and prepare them to be put in production, working closely with the team´s data analysts
Report to Top line TB Management and TB COO on all data queries and data needs
Contribute to the development and governance of our data and support the different Cash Management business line with data-driven decision-making
Meet different stakeholders to frame their data needs, based on your findings collect the necessary data, process the data, and turn it into meaningful tools meeting the requirements expressed by the teams
Participate and interact with inter-disciplinary teams such as IT, Business and Operations and contribute to the improvement and streamlining of data systems within the company to drive innovation


Profile And Skills To Success


Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Management, Business Intelligence, Business, Economics or Mathematics
Fluency in English
Highly proficient with MS Excel
Ability to quickly comprehend new software tools and learn how to use them optimally
Great attention to detail
Excellent interpersonal, communication and influence skills
Good conceptualization skills and data exposure
Open, curious and eager to learn and discover day to day banking products

Investment & Protection Services (IPS) combines a unique continuum of products and services across protection, saving, investment and real estate including specialised businesses offering a wide range of savings, investment and protection services

To ensure a comfortable and efficient working setup, eligible employees are provided with both office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional items at reduced prices.

  • Commitment towards work/life balance

Supporting employees in a hybrid way of working while providing them with the means to maintain a work-life balance is an essential dimension of our Smart Working program. At BNP Paribas, we care about our employees’ wellbeing and promote a culture of good integration between work and personal life.

To find out more on why you should join BNP Paribas please read our Employee Value Proposition and our Career path page.

  • Please note that only applications submitted in English will be considered.
  • In case you are selected for this role, further documentation will be requested to support your hiring process.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Expleo is a trusted partner for your innovation journey. As a global engineering, technology and consulting service provider, we are ideally positioned to help you achieve your ambitions and future-proof your business. With a smart blend of bold thinking and reliable execution, we’re able to fast-track innovation through each step of your value chain.

We are strategically positioned to build value, with a global footprint across 30 countries.

We are as global and local as you need us to be, with strong best-in-class pan-European technological centres and unique best-shoring capabilities.

We leverage a network of high value-adding affiliates in consulting and industrial excellence, and leading partners across multiple sectors to provide you with the most comprehensive services and solutions in an ever-changing environment.

Responsibilities


Perform manual data cleansing activities following defined survivorship logic and data quality rules.
Identify and resolve duplicate records across multiple datasets and systems.
Standardize names, addresses, and mandatory data fields according to established procedures.
Work with data from Excel files and local databases related to “Shadow IT” environments.
Structure, organize, and prepare legacy or fragmented data for migration activities.
Perform cross-system reconciliation between different platforms and datasets.
Compare CRM exports, SAP lists, and other data sources to identify discrepancies and data deltas.
Execute Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) accurately and consistently.
Follow global documentation and guidelines provided in English.


Essential Skills


Strong experience with manual data cleansing and data quality processes.
Experience handling data from multiple sources, including Excel files and local databases.
Ability to perform cross-system reconciliation and discrepancy analysis.
High attention to detail and accuracy when working with data.
Ability to follow SOPs and defined processes with precision.
Good English communication and comprehension skills.
Strong analytical and organizational skills.
Comfortable working with structured and unstructured datasets.


Desired Skills


Hands-on experience with Salesforce, Veeva, or SAP.
Basic SQL knowledge for data extraction and querying.
Scripting knowledge or automation experience.
Familiarity with Data Quality (DQ) tools and fuzzy-matching logic.
Experience working with AI-based data inference tools.
Previous experience working with external data providers such as IQVIA or OneKey.


What Do I Need Before I Apply


The opportunity is remote but the candidate must already be living in PT.

Title: Data Analyst Trainee - Real Assets
Description:
About The Job


Data is a strategic asset for BNP Paribas. It plays a fundamental role in the analysis of the teams’ performance and evolution ultimately allowing to become more efficient and to contribute to the sustainability of the business model and the accurate selection of the bank’s assets. To be in control of data is crucial to enhance risk management, to reach right credit decisions and bring added value to the business
Based in Lisbon, the Trainee will join a team of professionals who are known for their sectorial knowledge and support the team


Your Main Activities Are


Input missing data on credit files for presentation to the Credit Committee
Ensure accuracy of credit data
Ensure consistency of data between different credit files
Communicate with the CPM Credit Analyst Teams aiming to get updates on progress of credit files
Ensure proper understanding of credit files to identify structured data to be inputted
Create and maintain standard procedures for data input (user guides)
Cooperate and communicate with the teams in charge of the golden source systems to ensure correct data in the source system
Produce periodic reports based on data collected and captured, providing clear analysis and interpretation


Profile And Skills To Success


Bachelor’s Degree in Economics, Accounting or Management
Detail oriented with deep interest in data
Good knowledge of Excel
Good written/verbal communication skills in English (mandatory)
Ability to collaborate / teamwork
Efficient organizational and planning capacities
Self-starter, good initiative and flexible


About The Team


The Real Assets team includes a Coverage Relationship & Origination team, a Transaction & Execution team and a Credit Portfolio Management Team (CPM) spread across several countries serving the sector client in EMEA. These teams are split across Transportation (Shipping & Offshore, Aviation, Rail), Real Estate, Energy, Recourses & Infrastructure (ERI), Export, Leasing & Alternative Structuring. This transversality aims to provide a common training path, opportunities to explore other sectors, more efficiency and greater mobility


Why joining BNP Paribas?


Leading banking institution


BNP Paribas is the European Union’s leading bank, and key player in international banking. It operates in 63 countries and has nearly 183.000 employees, including more than 146.000 in Europe.


Our presence in Portugal


In Portugal since 1985, BNP Paribas today has more than 8.700 employees, distributed across the Group's 10 business entities established in the country. Its presence also extends to 11 excellence centers providing value-added services to various countries where the BNP Paribas Group also operates.


International reach


Thanks To Its International Presence And Regular And Close Collaboration Among Its Different Entities, BNP Paribas Has The Resources To Support All Clients With Financing, Investment, Savings And Protection Solutions That Help Make Their Projects a Success. BNP Paribas Holds Key Positions In Its Three Core Operating Divisions


Retail Banking, a division that brings together all of the Group’s retail activities and specialised business lines;
Investment & Protection Services that include specialised businesses offering a wide range of savings, investment and protection services;
Corporate & Institutional Banking division that offers tailored financial solutions for corporate and institutional clients.
Diversity and Inclusion commitment


BNP Paribas is an equal opportunity employer and proud to provide equal employment opportunity to all job seekers. We are actively committed to ensuring that no individual is discriminated against on the grounds of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage or civil partnership status, pregnancy and maternity/paternity, race, religion or belief, sex or sexual orientation. Equity and diversity are at the core of our recruitment policy because we believe that they foster creativity and efficiency, which in turn increase performance and productivity. We strive to reflect the society we live in, while keeping with the image of our clients.


Commitment towards work/life balance


At BNP Paribas we care about our employees wellbeing and promote a culture of good integration between work and rest. We believe our employees have rich personal lives outside of work, being fundamental to be disconnected from work to recharge both physically and mentally. Only through this balance we may all be at our best while working.


Remote Working Conditions


At BNP Paribas, we embrace a Smart Working framework based on trust, autonomy and collaboration. Within this framework, eligible employees can benefit from flexible remote working modalities adapted to our hybrid working environment. To guarantee a comfortable and efficient working set-up, eligible employees are provided with both the office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional equipment at reduced prices.

To find out more on why you should join BNP Paribas visit https://bnpp.lk/why-BNP-Paribas-Portugal


Please note that only applications submitted in English will be considered.
In case you are selected for this role, further documentation will be requested to support your hiring process.

Title: Performance Analyst | BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE
Description:
Country: Portugal

COMEÇA AQUI

O Santander (www.santander.com) está a evoluir de uma marca global de grande impacto para uma organização orientada pela tecnologia, e as nossas pessoas estão no centro desta jornada.

Juntos, estamos a liderar uma transformação centrada no cliente, que valoriza o pensamento crítico, a inovação e a coragem de desafiar o que é possível.

Esta é mais do que uma mudança estratégica. É uma oportunidade para pessoas motivadas crescerem, aprenderem e fazerem uma diferença real.

A nossa missão é contribuir para que mais pessoas e empresas prosperem. Adotámos uma cultura de risco sólida, e todas as nossas pessoas, em todos os níveis, têm um papel ativo e responsável na gestão de riscos.

O TEU IMPACTO

O Santander Portugal está à procura de um Performance Analyst para Lisboa.

Estamos a redefinir a forma como trabalhamos através da inovação, da tecnologia de ponta, da colaboração e da liberdade para explorar novas ideias.

Para ter sucesso nesta função, serás responsável por:


Participar no desenvolvimento de iniciativas de transformação e crescimento de negócio dentro da área de transformação digital, compreendendo e pensando o modelo de negócio com espírito crítico e identificando oportunidades de melhoria.
Dar apoio às squads de performance para resolver problemas, realizar análises de cliente e negócio e facilitar a tomada de decisão.
Criar dashboards para visualização de informação e geração de insights: relatórios de objetivos, tráfego por fontes, visualização de funis, conversões, etc., utilizando informação proveniente de diversas fontes (Google Analytics, Supermetrics, data lake, etc).
Avaliar a performance de campanhas e outras jornadas de comunicação e comerciais.
Colaborar com outras áreas da transformação digital.


O TEU CONTRIBUTO

As nossas pessoas são a nossa maior força. Cada indivíduo contribui com perspetivas únicas que nos tornam mais fortes como equipa e como organização.

Capacitamos as equipas para irem mais além, valorizando quem são e potenciando o que trazem consigo.

Os seguintes requisitos representam os conhecimentos, competências e capacidades essenciais para o sucesso nesta função. Serão consideradas adaptações razoáveis para permitir que pessoas com deficiência possam desempenhar as funções essenciais.

Experiência Profissional


Sólidos conhecimentos de Web Analytics, incluindo identificação e implementação de métricas, dimensões e segmentos.
Experiência em reporting e análise de funis de conversão (pageviews, eventos, e-commerce), análise de jornadas de utilizador e tracking/reporting de campanhas de Paid Media.


Formação Académica


Percurso académico em Gestão, Economia, Engenharia, Matemática, Ciência de Dados ou semelhante.


Idiomas


Português (fluente)
Inglês (preferencial)


Hard Skills


Sólidos conhecimentos de Web Analytics.
Certificação em ferramentas de web analytics, nomeadamente Google Analytics (valorizada experiência com GA4).
Conhecimentos de Excel (valorizada experiência com Power Query e Power Pivot) e ferramentas de visualização de dados (Power BI ou outras).


Soft Skills


Perfil analítico com forte capacidade de resolução de problemas.
Autonomia, rigor, proatividade e orientação para resultados.
Comunicação clara, empática e eficiente, adaptada aos diferentes stakeholders.
Capacidade para trabalhar num ambiente multidisciplinar e colaborativo.


VALORIZAMOS O TEU IMPACTO

A tua contribuição é importante e será reconhecida. Podes esperar um pacote de remuneração justo e competitivo, que reflete o impacto que crias e o valor que entregas.

Mas sabemos que as recompensas vão muito além dos números.


Oferecemos mais do que um salário. Os nossos benefícios foram concebidos para apoiar a tua vida, os teus objetivos e o teu bem‑estar, agora e no futuro.
A tua saúde é a nossa prioridade. Através do BeHealthy, o nosso programa global de bem‑estar, promovemos o bem‑estar holístico.
Sabemos que a família é tudo. Por isso, oferecemos licença parental, apoio à infância e programas adaptados a cada fase da vida.
Potenciamos as nossas equipas através de oportunidades globais e percursos de carreira diversificados.
Acreditamos no poder da ajuda. Pode dedicar parte do teu tempo de trabalho a atividades de voluntariado.


Estamos aqui para te manter motivado(a), ajudar‑te a alcançar os teus objetivos e celebrar o teu progresso em cada etapa do caminho.

CONFORMIDADE LOCAL

O Santander orgulha‑se de ser uma organização que promove a igualdade de oportunidades, independentemente da idade, género, deficiência, estado civil, raça, religião ou orientação sexual.

Comprometemo-nos a oferecer um processo de candidatura inclusivo e acessível a todas as pessoas

O QUE FAZER A SEGUIR

Se consideras que esta função é do teu interesse, candidata‑te.

Title: Analyst (Portugal)
Description:
About Cleerly

We’re Cleerly – a healthcare company that’s revolutionizing how heart disease is diagnosed, treated, and tracked. We were founded in 2017 by one of the world’s leading cardiologists and are a growing team of world-class engineering, operations, medical affairs, marketing, and sales leaders. We raised $223M in Series C funding in 2022 which has enabled rapid growth and continued support of our mission. In December 2024 we received an additional $106M in a Series C extension funding. Most of our teams work remotely and have access to our offices in Denver, Colorado, New, York, New York, Dallas, Texas, and Lisbon, Portugal with some roles requiring you to be on-site in a location.

Cleerly has created a new standard of care for heart disease through value-based, AI-driven precision diagnostic solutions with the goal of helping prevent heart attacks. Our technology goes beyond traditional measures of heart disease by enabling comprehensive quantification and characterization of atherosclerosis, or plaque buildup, in each of the heart arteries. Cleerly’s solutions are supported by more than a decade of performing some of the world’s largest clinical trials to identify important findings beyond symptoms that increase a person’s risk of heart attacks.

At Cleerly, we collaborate digitally and use a wide variety of systems. Our people use Google Workspace (GMail, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Slides), Slack, Confluence/Jira, and Zoom Video, prior experience in these areas is a plus. Role or department specific technology needs may vary and will be listed as requirements in the job description.

While we are mostly a remote company, travel is required for some team meetings and cross function projects typically once per month or once per quarter, for some roles like sales or external facing roles travel could be up to 90% of the time.

Cleerly is committed to providing safe and effective medical software that meets customer needs and our intended use. The adherence to all applicable regulatory and statutory requirements establishes a clear framework for setting measurable quality objectives. Our commitment to continually improving our products and processes proactively manages risks, ensuring ongoing compliance throughout the entire software lifecycle. Understanding this role's relevance and importance is critical to achieving Cleerly's quality objectives.

About The Opportunity

You will have the opportunity to develop the experience of planning and operationalize daily schedules, in order to accomplish the targets. Work in a different and growing innovative environment that mixes healthcare with AI in a market in expansion. Develop experience of working with a multicultural team local and remote.

Responsibilities


Analyze outputs generated by artificial intelligence from Coronary CTA exams.
Evaluate Coronary CTA exams for image quality.
Perform administrative duties as required.
Participation in continuous improvement projects
Comply with process and quality standards.


Requirements


Medical imaging and radiotherapy university Degree
Availability to work different shifts
High attention to detail
Great interpersonal skills - must be able to work well in a team environment
Desire and ability to learn new technologies - willing to learn
Coronary CTA or 3D post-processing experience is a plus


Salary:

€1460.00 (monthly)

Working at Cleerly takes HEART. Discover our Core Values:


H: Humility- be a servant leader
E: Excellence- deliver world-changing results
A: Accountability- do what you say; expect the same from others
R: Remarkable- inspire & innovate with impact
T: Teamwork- together we win


Don’t meet 100 percent of the qualifications? Apply anyway and help us diversify our candidate pool and workforce. We value experience, whether gained formally or informally on the job or through other experiences. Job duties, activities and responsibilities are subject to change by our company.

OUR COMPANY IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, protected veteran status, religion, physical or mental disability, marital status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, or any other basis protected by law, ordinance, or regulation.

For more information see our Privacy Policy (https://cleerlyhealth.com/privacy-policy). All official emails will come from @cleerlyhealth.com email accounts.

Cleerly is committed to providing safe and effective medical software that meets customer needs and our intended use. The adherence to all applicable regulatory and statutory requirements establishes a clear framework for setting measurable quality objectives. Our commitment to continually improving our products and processes proactively manages risks, ensuring ongoing compliance throughout the entire software lifecycle. Understanding this role's relevance and importance is critical to achieving Cleerly's quality objectives.

About The Opportunity

You will have the opportunity to develop the experience of planning and operationalize daily schedules, in order to accomplish the targets. Work in a different and growing innovative environment that mixes healthcare with AI in a market in expansion. Develop experience of working with a multicultural team local and remote.

Responsibilities

  • Analyze outputs generated by artificial intelligence from Coronary CTA exams.
  • Evaluate Coronary CTA exams for image quality.
  • Perform administrative duties as required.
  • Participation in continuous improvement projects
  • Comply with process and quality standards.

Requirements

  • Medical imaging and radiotherapy university Degree
  • Availability to work different shifts
  • High attention to detail
  • Great interpersonal skills - must be able to work well in a team environment
  • Desire and ability to learn new technologies - willing to learn
  • Coronary CTA or 3D post-processing experience is a plus

Salary:

€1460.00 (monthly)

Working at Cleerly takes HEART. Discover our Core Values:

  • H: Humility- be a servant leader
  • E: Excellence- deliver world-changing results
  • A: Accountability- do what you say; expect the same from others
  • R: Remarkable- inspire & innovate with impact
  • T: Teamwork- together we win

Don’t meet 100 percent of the qualifications? Apply anyway and help us diversify our candidate pool and workforce. We value experience, whether gained formally or informally on the job or through other experiences. Job duties, activities and responsibilities are subject to change by our company.

OUR COMPANY IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, protected veteran status, religion, physical or mental disability, marital status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, or any other basis protected by law, ordinance, or regulation.

For more information see our Privacy Policy (https://cleerlyhealth.com/privacy-policy). All official emails will come from @cleerlyhealth.com email accounts.

Title: Credit Data Analyst
Description:
About The Job


The Senior Credit Data Analyst performs continuous data analysis on the group's credit risk parameters and their impact on Risk Weight Assets calculation


Your Main Activities Are


Investigate, analyse and control the calculation of Risk Weight Assets
Monitor and explain Quarterly Variations
Participate in testing new implementations, gathering and documenting data on calculation or systems evolution
Collaborate with internal clients to better understand their needs towards the process and assure continuous improvement
Support, analyse and monitor the information provided for regulatory purposes
Ensure proper maintenance of documentation of all methodologies, tools, processes and procedures
Continuous participation in transversal projects, being a key participant for improvement and decision making


Profile And Skills To Success


Master's Degree in Economics, Finance or Accounting
At least 3 years of professional experience in risk and controls
Proficient in MS Office Microsoft Excel and MS Office Microsoft Access
Proficient in MySQL
Fluent in English, spoken and written
Good communication skills
Ability to collaborate and teamwork
Critical thinking
Proactivity
Analytical Ability


Why joining BNP Paribas?


Leading banking institution


Leading player in banking and financial services in Europe, BNP Paribas operates in 64 countries and has nearly 178,000 employees, including more than 144,000 in Europe


International reach


The Group holds key positions in several areas of banking and financial services that are structured around three operating divisions:


Corporate & Institutional Banking (CIB) connects companies needing financing and institutional clients looking for investment opportunities division that offers tailored financial solutions for corporate and institutional clients.
Commercial, Personal Banking & Services (CPBS) brings together all our commercial banks and several specialised businesses
Investment & Protection Services (IPS) combines a unique continuum of products and services across protection, saving, investment and real estate including specialised businesses offering a wide range of savings, investment and protection services
Our presence in Portugal


In Portugal since 1985, BNP Paribas today has more than 9.220 employees, distributed across the Group's 10 business entities established in the country. Its presence also extends to 11 centres of expertise providing value-added services to various countries where the BNP Paribas Group also operates.


Diversity and Inclusion commitment


BNP Paribas ´ambition Is To Cultivate An Open And Responsive Environment For All That Encourages Collaboration And Interaction. We Demonstrate Our Commitment By Actively Contributing To The Group’s Diversity And Inclusion Strategy And Goals, In Line With The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. As Part Of Our Local Corporate And Social Responsibility – Diversity & Inclusion Strategy, BNP Paribas Portugal Is Committed To


Promote a fair and unbiased recruitment process and offer professional development opportunities to all employees
Celebrate diversity and advocate for inclusion, both externally and internally, encouraging employee’s participation, creating space for different voices to be heard.


To foster the effort of BNP Paribas Portugal, multiple initiatives and events take place throughout the year where our people can find out more on the importance of diversity inclusion at the workplace and in our society.


Remote Working Conditions


At BNP Paribas, we embrace a Smart Working framework based on trust, autonomy and collaboration. Within this framework, eligible employees can benefit from flexible remote working options adapted to our hybrid working environment. To ensure a comfortable and efficient working setup, eligible employees are provided with both office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional items at reduced prices.


Commitment towards work/life balance


Supporting employees in a hybrid way of working while providing them with the means to maintain a work-life balance is an essential dimension of our Smart Working program. At BNP Paribas, we care about our employees’ wellbeing and promote a culture of good integration between work and personal life.

To find out more on why you should join BNP Paribas please read our Employee Value Proposition and our Career path page.


Please note that only applications submitted in English will be considered.
In case you are selected for this role, further documentation will be requested to support your hiring process.

Title: Data Analyst - Cost Office
Description:
About The Job

The Business Management Officer supports in the management of a group of teams, in all topics requiring transversal coordination or consolidation of information such as, for example, HR, Facilities, Finance or community management. They provide support to management processes of setting goals, planning and/or controlling the organization and leading the execution of any type of activity, such as a project or a process, being accountable for a wide variety of tasks requiring transversal coordination or consolidation and impacting the business. Topics may include analysis and elaboration of various reports and presentations used by Top Management in decision making process and focusing on continous improvement.

Your Main Activities Are


Support the leadership of the scope on their daily activities or projects
Perform research and actively support ongoing transversal projects and initiatives
Support to the deliverables and processes on Finance, HR, Operational Permanent Control, Business Continuity, Management, and other related to the management of a department within BNP Paribas
Develop and own data models with the purpose of consolidating data, analysing them and reporting them in a structured and articulated way to support and inform business decisions. Contribute to the development or maintenance of new data models in a large range of activities, such as budget monitoring, space management, monitoring of the recruitment, results of internal surveys, among others
Support in preparing workshops or meetings with internal clients
Support in managing the internal communities, with the organization of events, management of internal sites, sharing of information via mailing list
Support in following-up and implementing continuous improvement or new projects that benefit and improve the management of the scope
Support to maintain the knowledge management and audit trail processes
Support in the yearly budget process and in following-up the budget
Support in the management of the portfolio of projects within its scope of teams


Profile and Skills To Success


Bachelor Degree in Management or Engineering or any similar areas
Up to 2 years of experience on Data Analytics and Reporting
Fluency in English, both oral and written
Experience using Python and Power BI
Great verbal and written communication skills and organizational skills
Reliability and capacity to deliver results with strict deadlines, polyvalent, flexible and able to adapt

Title: Data Analyst (BI & Analytics)
Description:
Every moment counts. Especially those you live to the fullest. Welcome to Consort Group.

For over 30 years, Consort Group has been helping companies leverage their data and infrastructure. It is backed by two leaders, Consortis and Consortia, and places people and social responsibility at the heart of its values.

This is your future team

Established in 2021, Consort Portugal supports the strategic offshoring choices of our clients, particularly in Europe, and brings our expertise in infrastructure services to the Portuguese market. With a team of around 45 employees, our Service Center based in Porto is developing a dynamic nearshore business, offering the French and European markets an environment of excellence and high potential.

Data Analyst (BI & Analytics)

This is your mission

Are you passionate about data analysis and business intelligence? Then this position is for you.

As Data Analyst (BI & Analytics), you will be responsible for analysis, development, and support activities within BI projects, working closely with data and production teams.

Build Side


Analyze, develop, test, and integrate solutions for BI & Analytics projects
Contribute to reporting solutions using Power BI
Align application code with technical and methodological practices
Work with teams to support self-service BI environments
Participate in Agile methodologies (Kanban, Lean, Scrum)


Run Side


Coordinate with production teams for releases
Ensure maintenance and level 3 support of applications
Monitor and improve application performance
Support business users and resolve issues
Ensure continuity of BI services


This is your background

You have experience in data analysis and BI environments, working with reporting tools and databases.

This is your career path.

You have contributed to BI and analytics projects, combining development, reporting, and support activities in Agile environments.

This Is Your Expertise


Power BI (reporting)
SQL
PL/SQL


Nice To Have


SAP Business Objects
Python
Tibco Spotfire
Dataiku
Alteryx


This is how you work as part of a team:


Autonomous and organized
Delivery-focused with attention to detail
Proactive in risk mitigation
Ability to work in multicultural environments
Ability to work under pressure and tight deadlines
Strong motivation and work ethic


Language Skills


English – Expert
Portuguese – Mastery
French – Notions (nice to have)


This is our commitment

At Consort Group, you are an expert who we support so that every assignment becomes a step that counts.


Attentive and human onboarding
A truly hands-on management style
Continuous training opportunities
Concrete commitments: inclusion, equality, solidarity
A comprehensive HR package: health insurance, TR card, CSE, pension plan
A culture of feedback and meaningful projects


The Recruitment Process


An initial phone call with our recruitment team
An HR interview and a job interview with a business engineer
A test or technical interview with one of our experts
A final meeting with your future manager or project manager
And if we're a good fit, we'll get started together


Good to know

Location: Lisbon

Contract: Permanent contract / B2B

Remote work: Hybrid

Job category: Data / BI / Analytics

What you will do here, you won't do anywhere else.

This moment is yours.

Title: Data Products Analyst (Associate Product Owner)
Description:
At Bose Corporation, we believe sound is the most powerful force on earth — and for over 60 years, we have been a company built on innovation, excellence, and independence. Privately owned, fiercely customer-focused, and driven by our values, we continue to lead industries and transform lives through sound.

Today, Bose Corporation is entering an exciting new era. Across multiple global Business Units and Global Functions, we are shaping the future of audio technology, automotive, luxury, and premium experiences. We invite you to join us in this transformation.

Job Description

We are looking for an Associate Product Owner to support Portfolio Strategy & Operations within a domain. This role focuses on day-to-day delivery mechanics - backlog readiness, coordination, documentation, and operational rigor—so the team can deliver consistent, high-quality data products.

It’s an ideal early-career role for someone who wants to grow into end-to-end product ownership, building deep domain expertise and learning how to translate business priorities into scalable data products that drive real impact.

You will partner with Senior Product Owners, delivery teams (Analytics Engineering, BI/Insights, Data Science, NLP), and business stakeholders to translate needs into well-defined work, support releases, and help drive adoption through feedback loops.

Responsibilities


Own backlog hygiene and readiness: draft epics/user stories, clarify acceptance criteria, and ensure work is delivery-ready for execution sprints.
Coordinate delivery mechanics: support refinement, planning, demos, and working sessions; track actions, dependencies, and risks (RAID).
Collaborate closely with technical teams (Data Engineering, Analytics Engineering, BI/Insights, Data Science/NLP) to clarify requirements, manage dependencies, and ensure solutions are feasible, high-quality, and delivered on time
Support demand shaping by gathering inputs, documenting workflows, and preparing problem statements and success measures for Sr PO review.
Coordinate release readiness and change enablement artifacts (release notes, training inputs, UAT coordination, rollout checklists).
Capture post-release feedback and product health signals; maintain an improvement backlog and support iteration cycles.
Support AI/GenAI pilots and adoption readiness (test plans, user feedback capture, documentation) to help move intelligent capabilities from prototype to practical use.


Qualifications

Required:


Demonstrated ability to translate business needs into structured requirements, epics, or user stories with clear acceptance criteria.
Experience supporting data/analytics projects or products - working with datasets, KPIs/metrics, reporting/BI, and partnering with technical teams to deliver data-driven solutions
Experience coordinating cross-functional work (product operations, business analysis, project/program support, analytics delivery, or similar).
Strong interest in data, analytics, and AI-enabled products; comfortable learning new tools and concepts quickly.
Excellent organization and communication skills; ability to coordinate stakeholders and keep work moving.
Comfort working in an agile environment and using common delivery tools (e.g., Jira/Confluence or equivalents).


Preferred


Working knowledge of SQL and ability to validate outputs with analysts/engineers
Experience with BI & Data Science tools (e.g., Power BI, Data Bricks, AWS) and/or analytics engineering concepts (e.g., dbt, semantic layers).
Familiarity with AI/GenAI concepts or tools (e.g., prompting, copilots, NLP), and interest in responsible AI practices.
Experience supporting business process improvement or shared service workflows in any function.


Are you interested in this position?

We’d love to know you better! Submit your application, by uploading your CV in English via the “Apply” button above.

For further information about the position please contact us via ITCareers_Portugal@bose.com.

‎

‎

Our goal is to create an atmosphere where every candidate feels supported and empowered in the interviewing process. Diversity and inclusion are integral to our success, and we believe that providing reasonable accommodation is not only a legal obligation but also a fundamental aspect of our commitment to being an employer of choice. We recognize that individuals may have different needs and requirements based on their abilities, and we provide reasonable accommodations to ensure ideal conditions are met during the application process.

  • Experience coordinating cross-functional work (product operations, business analysis, project/program support, analytics delivery, or similar).
  • Strong interest in data, analytics, and AI-enabled products; comfortable learning new tools and concepts quickly.
  • Excellent organization and communication skills; ability to coordinate stakeholders and keep work moving.
  • Comfort working in an agile environment and using common delivery tools (e.g., Jira/Confluence or equivalents).

Preferred

  • Working knowledge of SQL and ability to validate outputs with analysts/engineers
  • Experience with BI & Data Science tools (e.g., Power BI, Data Bricks, AWS) and/or analytics engineering concepts (e.g., dbt, semantic layers).
  • Familiarity with AI/GenAI concepts or tools (e.g., prompting, copilots, NLP), and interest in responsible AI practices.
  • Experience supporting business process improvement or shared service workflows in any function.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Every moment counts. Especially those you live to the fullest. Welcome to Consort Group.

For over 30 years, Consort Group has been helping companies leverage their data and infrastructure. It is backed by two leaders, Consortis and Consortia, and places people and social responsibility at the heart of its values.

This is your future team

Established in 2021, Consort Portugal supports the strategic offshoring choices of our clients, particularly in Europe, and brings our expertise in infrastructure services to the Portuguese market. With a team of around 45 employees, our Service Center based in Porto is developing a dynamic nearshore business, offering the French and European markets an environment of excellence and high potential.

Data Analyst

This is your mission

Are you passionate about data, analytics, and turning complex datasets into actionable insights? Then this position is for you.

As a Data Analyst, you will be responsible for delivering data-driven insights and analytical solutions within a quantitative data intelligence environment, directly supporting business stakeholders in global markets.

Build Side


You will contribute to the design, development, and industrialization of data solutions:
Collect, transform, and structure data from multiple sources (ETL pipelines)
Build and maintain reliable datasets and data models for analysis
Develop automated reporting solutions and dashboards
Participate in data warehouse structuring and optimization
Design and implement analytical datasets using SQL and Python
Support the development of APIs and data pipelines when needed
Ensure data quality, consistency, and scalability of solutions

Run Side


You will ensure the operational reliability and continuous improvement of data solutions:
Monitor data flows, dashboards, and reporting processes
Troubleshoot and resolve data issues in collaboration with technical teams
Support business users (front office stakeholders) with data insights
Maintain and enhance existing dashboards and analytical tools
Optimize performance of data processing workflows
Contribute to automation and industrialization of recurring analyses
Ensure documentation and knowledge sharing across teams

This is your background

You have at least 2 years of experience in data analysis or a similar analytical environment. You enjoy working with data, solving complex problems, and continuously improving processes. You are looking for an environment where your ideas matter and where technology and business work closely together.

This is your career path

You are eager to grow in a data-driven environment, bridging technical and business worlds. You are motivated by impactful analytical work, exposure to financial markets, and the opportunity to work on end-to-end data pipelines and visualization solutions.

This Is Your Expertise


Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or related fields
Strong experience in data analysis using Python (Pandas) and SQL
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or Power BI
Good understanding of data manipulation and reporting concepts
Knowledge of descriptive statistics (hypothesis testing is a plus)
Exposure to Big Data tools (PySpark, Airflow, Presto) is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus
Familiarity with data modeling and ETL processes


This is how you work as part of a team:


Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset
Ability to translate business needs into technical solutions
Clear and concise communication with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Comfortable working in agile and collaborative environments
Proactive, curious, and driven by automation and continuous improvement
Ability to handle multiple tasks and prioritize effectively


Language Skills


English: C1


This is our commitment

At Consort Group, you are an expert who we support so that every assignment becomes a step that counts.


Attentive and human onboarding
A truly hands-on management style
Continuous training opportunities
Concrete commitments: inclusion, equality, solidarity
A comprehensive HR package: health insurance, TR card, CSE, pension plan
A culture of feedback and meaningful projects


The Recruitment Process


An initial phone call with our recruitment team
An HR interview and a job interview with a business engineer
A test or technical interview with one of our experts
A final meeting with your future manager or project manager
And if we're a good fit, we'll get started together


Good to know

Location: Lisbon or Porto

Contract: Permanent contract

Remote work: Hybrid (50%)

Job category: Data / Analytics / Quantitative Data Intelligence

What you will do here, you won't do anywhere else.

This moment is yours.

Title: Client Development Data Analyst
Description:
Keywords: Global Markets, Client Development, Data Analytics, Client Strategy

About The Job

Join our global team to turn data into actionable insights for client strategies in global markets. You’ll work closely with the regional hubs to analyze trends, automate processes, and improve decision-making using internal and external data, ultimately supporting the sales organization. This role blends analytics, problem-solving, project management and collaboration to drive growth for our global client franchise.

Your Main Activities Are


Analyze client data to identify trends, opportunities, and performance gaps for global sales teams
Design and track key metrics (KPIs) to measure client segmentation and strategy success
Build or improve tools (e.g., dashboards, reports) to simplify workflows and support data-driven decisions
Automate repetitive tasks (e.g., data cleaning, reporting) to scale analytics and reduce manual work
Partner with regional teams (US, UK, France, Hong Kong) to simplify day-to-day operations and facilitate the implementation of our client strategy
Translate business needs into technical requirements for developers and present insights to non-technical stakeholders
Support ad-hoc requests like data extracts, presentations, or process improvements
Ensure high-quality client data across systems to enable accurate analysis


Profile And Skills To Success


Degree in Finance, Data Analysis, Business Intelligence, Engineering, Mathematics, or related fields.
2+ years of hands-on experience with Excel, Python, and data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau).
Experience with SQL, Power BI, Power Query and DAX is a plus
Strong problem-solving skills to turn complex data into clear recommendations
Ability to prioritize tasks and work in a fast-changing, hybrid environment (Lisbon/Porto office).
Fluent English (written and verbal) to collaborate with global teams
Ability to manage projects
Strong communication skills, to build strong relationships with stakeholders and partners
Global Markets or Investment Banking experience is preferred


About The Team

Part of BNP Paribas’ Global Markets, this team supports client strategy through data analytics, market intelligence, and digital solutions. We work across regions to:


Define client segmentation and track performance with KPIs
Streamline processes (e.g., broker reviews, governance forums) using data and automation
Bridge the gap between technical teams and business stakeholders to drive global consistency


Global Markets, the capital markets division of BNP Paribas' Corporate & Institutional Banking, is a powerhouse delivering top-tier solutions across asset classes. Our Portugal Platform (Porto and Lisbon) stands as a major European HUB and a unique player in the country, boasting a wide range of Front Office roles and services – Trading, Sales, Research, Structuring, Quants & Engineering, Business Dev and Support, Marketing & Client Support, Risk Management, Data Analytics and Machine Learning. With rapid growth and ambitions to make a global impact, we offer numerous short and long-term opportunities.

Get a glimpse into our world- Check out our Global Markets Portugal webpage.

Why joining BNP Paribas?


Leading banking institution


Leading player in banking and financial services in Europe, BNP Paribas operates in 64 countries and has nearly 178,000 employees, including more than 144,000 in Europe


International reach


The Group holds key positions in several areas of banking and financial services that are structured around three operating divisions:


Corporate & Institutional Banking (CIB) connects companies needing financing and institutional clients looking for investment opportunities division that offers tailored financial solutions for corporate and institutional clients.
Commercial, Personal Banking & Services (CPBS) brings together all our commercial banks and several specialised businesses
Investment & Protection Services (IPS) combines a unique continuum of products and services across protection, saving, investment and real estate including specialised businesses offering a wide range of savings, investment and protection services
Our presence in Portugal


In Portugal since 1985, BNP Paribas today has more than 9.220 employees, distributed across the Group's 10 business entities established in the country. Its presence also extends to 11 centres of expertise providing value-added services to various countries where the BNP Paribas Group also operates.


Diversity and Inclusion commitment


BNP Paribas ´ambition Is To Cultivate An Open And Responsive Environment For All That Encourages Collaboration And Interaction. We Demonstrate Our Commitment By Actively Contributing To The Group’s Diversity And Inclusion Strategy And Goals, In Line With The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. As Part Of Our Local Corporate And Social Responsibility – Diversity & Inclusion Strategy, BNP Paribas Portugal Is Committed To


Promote a fair and unbiased recruitment process and offer professional development opportunities to all employees
Celebrate diversity and advocate for inclusion, both externally and internally, encouraging employee’s participation, creating space for different voices to be heard.


To foster the effort of BNP Paribas Portugal, multiple initiatives and events take place throughout the year where our people can find out more on the importance of diversity inclusion at the workplace and in our society.


Remote Working Conditions


At BNP Paribas, we embrace a Smart Working framework based on trust, autonomy and collaboration. Within this framework, eligible employees can benefit from flexible remote working options adapted to our hybrid working environment. To ensure a comfortable and efficient working setup, eligible employees are provided with both office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional items at reduced prices.


Commitment towards work/life balance


Supporting employees in a hybrid way of working while providing them with the means to maintain a work-life balance is an essential dimension of our Smart Working program. At BNP Paribas, we care about our employees’ wellbeing and promote a culture of good integration between work and personal life.

To find out more on why you should join BNP Paribas please read our Employee Value Proposition and our Career path page.


Please note that only applications submitted in English will be considered.
In case you are selected for this role, further documentation will be requested to support your hiring process.

Title: Business Intelligence TMK Analyst
Description:
About The Role

As our Business Intelligence TKM Analyst, you will step into a central position where analytical excellence meets real business impact. You will support the execution of All Inclusive Contracts, ensuring full compliance with our Pay for Performance principles while turning complex data into actionable insights for multiple departments. Additionally, you will be working with both internal and external sources to enhance our understanding of market dynamics, helping the commercial organization make smarter, faster decisions. With a strong focus on Power BI development, continuous improvement of reporting tools, and project collaboration on MYPS (My Perfect Store), this is a role where you can shape the way we measure and elevate performance across the business.

Main Responsibilities:

In this position, you will oversee the tracking and audit of All Inclusive Contracts, ensuring in‑store activities are properly implemented and transparently monitored throughout the year. You will handle ongoing Channel Management responsibilities, refreshing and designing standard reports that make large data sets meaningful and business‑ready. Daily interactions with external data suppliers will keep you at the center of Data Quality Management, while your support to Sales, Trade Marketing and Marketing will help them unlock deeper insights through your analysis. Furthermore, you will drive the tracking and closure of Plan Trade activities and contribute to Bimonthly, Seasonal and weekly reporting routines. Alongside this, your role as a Power BI key user will allow you to develop richer dashboards and enhance reporting capabilities in close collaboration with the IT team. Lastly, your involvement in MYPS and additional analytical projects will ensure successful monitoring of performance in the impulse and broader FMCG environment.

About You:

This role is perfect for someone who thrives in a dynamic FMCG environment and truly enjoys transforming data into business stories. You bring strong analytical skills, high attention to detail and a proactive mindset that drives continuous improvement. Experience with Business Intelligence, Power BI, Retail Analytics or FMCG data sources (such as sell‑out or in‑store execution data) will help you succeed from day one. A natural collaborator, you understand how to support commercial teams, communicate insights clearly and manage multiple stakeholders. Most importantly, you are curious, solution‑oriented and excited to influence business decisions through high-quality insights and reliable reporting foundations.

Our Benefits & Perks:

Careers with caring built in - discover our benefits here.

About Ferrero:

Ferrero began its journey in the small town of Alba in Piedmont, Italy, in 1946. Today, it is one of the world’s largest sweet-packaged food companies, with many iconic brands sold in countries all over the world. Find out more about Ferrero at ferrero.com.

DE&I at Ferrero:

Ferrero is committed to building a diverse and inclusive culture in which all employees feel welcomed and appreciated and have the same opportunities. We believe all our people are equally talented in their own way. In nurturing the curiosity and natural abilities of our employees, we provide them, generation after generation, the means to succeed personally and professionally, enabling them to craft their journey at Ferrero. The diversity of our talents is what makes our work environment multicultural, innovative, and highly rewarding.

Find out more here.

Title: Data Engineer
HTML:
At Axians, we value talent, not labels.

We believe in a culture of inclusion, where everyone has a place and all applications are considered based on merit, without discrimination.

This is your opportunity to join an international group with a project that needs you to help meet the challenges of digital transformation.

💻 THE ROLE

We are looking for a #TechTalent to work as a Data Engineer for a national project.

🙂 YOUR DAY TO DAY

  • Develop the technical solution framework and participate in TPCV modeling ;
  • Develop the TPCV and implement unit tests;
  • Industrialize the solution according to CI/CD best practices;
  • Participate in the validation phase with end-users;
  • Implement the data exposure solution.

💡 What We're Looking For

  • Proficiency in Python programming language and libraries such as Pandas, Polars, SQLAlchemy, Psycopg2, ibmboto3, etc.;
  • Mastery of good programming practices (CI/CD, unit testing, versioning, etc.) and Dataiku;
  • Experience with cloud environments (AWS, IBM, etc.) and containerization using Docker;
  • Agile Methodologies and tools (Jira, Confluence, ALM);
  • Knowledge of ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes and best practices/optimizations ;
  • Strong understanding of SQL and one or more databases (PostgreSQL, Oracle, etc.);
  • Fluency in English (written & spoken), proficiency in French is a plus.

⭐ What You Can Expect From Us

Stable professional career

Working with Axians projects becomes an asset to those of us who want to offer stability through a secure career.

Prospects for career advancement

You can access training and new certifications that will further elevate your skills. The 360° evaluation from managers and peers will also allow you to evolve.

Work Life Balance

By collaborating with Axians, you will be able to reconcile work and family requirements without neglecting either area.

Attractive, Evolving Compensation

When working with Axians, you will have access to performance bonuses in addition to an attractive salary pack (base salary + benefits). You are also entitled to health insurance and an employee assistance program for you and your family (Psychological, Legal, Psychosocial, and Financial support).

Do you identify with this position?

If you think this position is the right fit for you and you have the #TechTalent we are looking for, send us your resume, preferably in English and in Europass format, to recrutamento.axianspt@axians.com with the subject “DE/TP/DB”.

Learn more about Axians

Title: Financial Data / System Analyst Trainee - Lisbon
Description:
About The Job


Within Finance & Strategy function (F&S), Finance Implementation Projects (FIP): Lead the end-to-end implementation of business and regulatory projects to meet Business Line and Finance requirements; Contribute to transformation initiatives according to the Finance global strategy; Implement and maintain a harmonized Finance operating model across BNP Paribas Group; Contribute to migration projects relying on the regional set up in Europe, Asia and Americas; Collaborate with FIP community worldwide, ensuring a consistent approach regarding Finance projects across all geographies
FIP teams deliver high quality projects for the entire BNP Paribas Group, impacting multiple business lines activity, from Retail to Corporate and Investment Banking, among others
The Business Analyst Trainee for Finance Projects joining FIP Portugal will follow a complete integration program for one year combining periods of effective training and project activity
The aim of FIP Integration Program is to support the collaborators during their first period when joining our team, constantly developing the knowledge about the Group and our activity, preparing them for their current challenge but also aligned with a career path within Finance & Strategy
The Financial Data / System Analyst Trainee for Finance projects will assist senior members within the team and contribute to the implementation phase of the projects


Your Main Activities Are


Analyse the requirements from Business Line or Finance (depending on the nature of the project) based on the framing note of the FIP Project Manager
Perform technical gap analysis and technical impact study in order to contribute to the framing note delivered by FIP Project Manager
Collaborate with FIP Business Analyst to organize and manage meetings/workshops with stakeholders within Finance project team, Business Lines, Information Technology (IT), Finance end users to design the technical solution of the project respecting methodology and standards
Document the technical specifications of the solution to be set up in the financial accounting interpreter
Parametrize accounting and reporting rules, according to the standards in terms of norms and systems
Contribute to the testing strategy to ensure the quality of the implementation of the project
Carry out all unitary tests and integration tests of the project
Ensure an aftercare support for Finance end users after delivery of the solution
Support Project Managers and Business Analysts in implementing project outcomes and follow up outcomes with stakeholders
Participate to project committees by sharing actions and decisions status
Facilitate the knowledge sharing within the team
Support transversal processes on business activity
Support administrative processes in new joiners onboarding
Contribute to documentation review
Contribute to efficiency with process automation


Profile And Skills To Success


Master’s degree or Engineering school degree completed
Knowledge of accounting or finance
Knowledge of financial Information Systems
Proficient in Microsoft Office pack (Outlook, Word, Excel, Access, PowerPoint)
Basic knowledge of inquiry tools (Business Objects)
Beginner knowledge or understanding of Application Integration Server (AIS)/Real‑time Data Integration (RDJ) Axway software and/or mainframe Multiple Virtual Storage (MVS)
Fluent in English, spoken and written
Teamwork and ability to collaborate
Rigor and attention to detail
Good organizational skills
Good communication skills
Analytical ability


About The Team


The F&S function ensures, for the BNP Paribas Group, the production and publication of accounting and financial management statements necessary for the management of the Group, financial and tax optimization along with the external growth strategy. It also plays an alert role vis-à-vis of the General Management
F&S count +1800 employees specialized in Accounting, Reporting, Performance Steering, Tax, Control, Regulatory and Project to mention few of them. F&S is organized around 3 mutualized regional platform (Europe, America and Asia/Pacific), 1 Shared Finance Service centre located in India, Central F&S teams located in Paris and decentralized Finance, Tax and Development departments located in each BNP Paribas country presence
The Infinity Europe, Middle East, Africa (EMEA) Finance & Strategy Platform is the Regional EMEA Mutualized platform and Group platform delivering services that covers the whole financial dimension: Accounting production activities, reporting, control, transformation projects, IT and GDO. Our platform serves EMEA Countries and Chief Financial Officer´s, Group Head Office as well as worldwide entities when it refers to projects (Transformations and IT). The Infinity EMEA Finance & Strategy Platform is in 2 different locations: Portugal and Spain


Why joining BNP Paribas?


Leading banking institution


Leading player in banking and financial services in Europe, BNP Paribas operates in 64 countries and has nearly 178,000 employees, including more than 144,000 in Europe


International reach


The Group holds key positions in several areas of banking and financial services that are structured around three operating divisions:


Corporate & Institutional Banking (CIB) connects companies needing financing and institutional clients looking for investment opportunities division that offers tailored financial solutions for corporate and institutional clients
Commercial, Personal Banking & Services (CPBS) brings together all our commercial banks and several specialised businesses heard
Investment & Protection Services (IPS) combines a unique continuum of products and services across protection, saving, investment and real estate including specialised businesses offering a wide range of savings, investment and protection services
Our presence in Portugal


In Portugal since 1985, BNP Paribas today has more than 9.220 employees, distributed across the Group's 10 business entities established in the country. Its presence also extends to 11 centres of expertise providing value-added services to various countries where the BNP Paribas Group also operates.


Diversity and Inclusion commitment


BNP Paribas ´ambition Is To Cultivate An Open And Responsive Environment For All That Encourages Collaboration And Interaction. We Demonstrate Our Commitment By Actively Contributing To The Group’s Diversity And Inclusion Strategy And Goals, In Line With The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. As Part Of Our Local Corporate And Social Responsibility – Diversity & Inclusion Strategy, BNP Paribas Portugal Is Committed To


Promote a fair and unbiased recruitment process and offer professional development opportunities to all employees
Celebrate diversity and advocate for inclusion, both externally and internally, encouraging employee’s participation, creating space for different voices to be heard.


To foster the effort of BNP Paribas Portugal, multiple initiatives and events take place throughout the year where our people can find out more on the importance of diversity inclusion at the workplace and in our society.

At BNP Paribas, we embrace a Smart Working framework based on trust, autonomy and collaboration. Within this framework, eligible employees can benefit from flexible remote working options adapted to our hybrid working environment. To ensure a comfortable and efficient working setup, eligible employees are provided with both office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional items at reduced prices.

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
We are Kaizen Gaming

Kaizen Gaming, the team powering Betano, is one of the biggest GameTech companies in the world, operating in 20 markets. We always aim to leverage cutting-edge technology, providing the best experience to our millions of customers who trust us for their entertainment.

We are a diverse team of more than 2.700 Kaizeners, from 40+ nationalities spreading across 3 continents.

Our #oneteam is proud to be among the Best Workplaces in Europe and certified Great Place to Work across our offices. Here, there’ll be no average day for you. Ready to Press Play on Potential?

Let's start with the role

As a Product Analyst on the Sportsbook Product team, you will help shape how product performance is measured by defining the right questions to ask, and scaling analytics methods and tools to support our growing business. You will drive high-quality, high-velocity decisions for Sportsbook’s diverse product teams while directly reporting to our Head of Sportsbook Product.

We’re looking for an experienced product analyst to be responsible for defining and tracking KPIs, supporting strategic initiatives, evaluating A/B tests through 3rd-party experimentation platforms, and aggregating data from multiple sources—from GA4 to our big data lake of transactional data—to help the team achieve a 360° view of our customers.

Our ideal candidate is a great storyteller, using data to support product roadmaps and ensure that any new feature launched is continually improved through data insights and impactful narratives. Your input will be crucial in decision-making across all major product strategies and initiatives for new feature development and ongoing product improvements.

As a Product Analyst, you will:


Work alongside the Sportsbook Product Team to support our dynamic and fast-paced Product roadmap, providing insights to help achieve our Product vision by defining, monitoring and reporting on KPIs and product metrics.
Leverage the transactional data lake to connect customer journeys with business outcomes.
Support our experimentation process by analyzing A/B test results and helping expand our internal experimentation framework.
Implement GA4 tagging to map all user journeys, identify and highlight customer pain points across products, and work closely with the Sportsbook product management team to prioritize these in the roadmap.
Synthesize data from a variety of sources, in close collaboration with BI and other stakeholders, to develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations (e.g., funnels in Looker Studio or other internal tools) that drive understanding and inform action across the Sportsbook vertical.
Ensure data quality, consistency, and governance across all analytics initiatives to maintain trust and reliability in insights.
Use data analytics, data visualization, and data modeling techniques to extract actionable insights that will facilitate prioritization and product strategy formulation.
Advise and inform the Sportsbook team at each stage of the product lifecycle—from supporting product and design teams during discovery to measuring the impact of features post-launch—while collaborating closely with Product Managers, Engineers, Designers, BI Analysts, and Product Analysts from other divisions.


What you’ll bring:

A great candidate for this role is passionate about working in a fast-paced product environment and is eager to enhance our product offering while creating an exceptional betting experience.


2+ years of product analytics experience in a consumer internet business, or within a high-velocity, high-growth product or business unit.
Experience assessing the impact of A/B experiments in a fast-paced product development cycle, ideally using platforms such as Kameleoon or Optimizely.
Proficiency in SQL for data manipulation.
Ability to independently determine and establish data analysis priorities, balancing ongoing projects with requests from various stakeholders.
Experience with GA4 and other product or experience analytics platforms, with strong skills in creating dashboards in Looker Studio or similar visualization tools.
Strong written communication skills to share insights and influence stakeholders.
Detail-oriented, impact-driven, customer-centric approach to data analysis.


Nice to have:


Previous experience in the sports betting industry.
Experience with BI/visualization tools such as PowerBI.
Familiarity with Databricks or similar big data platforms.


Kaizen Gaming Perks


🕑 Hybrid way of working;
🏃 A buddy will support you with your onboarding;
💸 Competitive pay & bonus scheme;
⭐Developmental 360° feedback framework;
💰 Monthly meal allowance;
👩‍⚕️ Private health insurance for you and your family;
📚 Unlimited access to Udemy & continuous training;
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family Support.


Recruitment Privacy Notice

Regarding the data you share with us, you may find and read our recruitment privacy notice here.

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of race, gender, religion, sexual orientation,or age.
