Title: Senior Data Science Analyst - OptimusAI
Description:
Who You'll Work With

Driving lasting impact and building long-term capabilities with our clients is not easy work. You are the kind of person who thrives in a high performance/high reward culture - doing hard things, picking yourself up when you stumble, and having the resilience to try another way forward.

In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we'll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities you need to become a stronger leader faster than you ever thought possible. Your colleagues—at all levels—will invest deeply in your development, just as much as they invest in delivering exceptional results for clients. Every day, you'll receive apprenticeship, coaching, and exposure that will accelerate your growth in ways you won’t find anywhere else.

When you join us, you will have:


Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions for our clients. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
World-class benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.


Your Impact

As a Data Science Specialist, you’ll collaborate in a dynamic, multi-disciplinary team of 3–6 experts, using your creativity and analytics expertise to drive client performance in areas like energy efficiency, product quality, and capacity. You’ll work side-by-side with client process engineers and analytics specialists, presenting your insights directly to executive audiences.

Internally, you’ll partner with our talented product engineers to develop innovative products and algorithms that address both current and future client needs. You’ll play a key role in codifying best practices, amplifying our impact both inside and outside the firm, and shaping the product roadmap.

You’ll be based in our Brussels office as part of McKinsey’s manufacturing AI solution, OptimusAI, which sits at the intersection of three practices: Global Energy and Materials, QuantumBlack, and Operations.

Global Energy and Materials: Advises production and investor companies across chemicals & agriculture, paper & packaging, metals & mining, oil & gas, and power generation on strategic, operational, and growth issues.

QuantumBlack, AI by McKinsey: The AI arm of our firm, helping clients transform faster, innovate smarter, and anticipate the future.

Operations: Assists clients in solving complex operational challenges by developing and implementing strategies and solutions for their most critical problems—connecting boardroom strategies to shop floor operators.

At the intersection of these groups, OptimusAI is a team of ~10 expert consultants, data scientists, and engineers focused on improving processing plant productivity through data analytics. We develop real-time AI advisory systems to support decision making in plant control rooms, optimize process control systems, and unlock plant bottlenecks through data-driven process changes.

OptimusAI also supports a global community of consultants by developing an innovative machine learning toolkit and codifying our approach to deliver significant and lasting client impact.

You’ll benefit from world-class training in business problem structuring and client advisory, working closely with senior manufacturing and analytics experts. Experience unparalleled career growth, with significant ownership and responsibility from day one, all within a collaborative, diverse, and non-hierarchical environment. Plus, seize the opportunity to travel to client sites—locally and around the world.

There is a flexibility to hire at different role level, depending on your experience.

Your Qualifications and Skills


Bachelor’s or master’s degree in data science, computer science, engineering, or a related field
3+ years of hands-on experience in advanced analytics, ideally within heavy industry environments
Advanced Python programming skills, with a passion for writing clean, efficient, modular, and maintainable code
Proven ability to implement and maintain MLOps pipelines for seamless model integration and deployment
Strong DevOps and backend engineering capabilities, including experience with CI/CD pipelines, containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and infrastructure as code (Terraform, Ansible)
Self-starter with excellent organizational abilities—able to independently initiate tasks and drive them to completion
Skilled in making rational, data-driven decisions based on facts and logical reasoning
Ability to create high-quality work products, such as PowerPoint presentations, Excel models, articles, or other written outputs
Exceptional time management skills to thrive in a complex, largely autonomous work environment
Willingness to work or attend meetings outside traditional business hours, and to take on projects at short notice when needed
Outstanding verbal and written communication skills in English with the ability to tailor your style to different audiences and seniority levels

In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we'll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities you need to become a stronger leader faster than you ever thought possible. Your colleagues—at all levels—will invest deeply in your development, just as much as they invest in delivering exceptional results for clients. Every day, you'll receive apprenticeship, coaching, and exposure that will accelerate your growth in ways you won’t find anywhere else.

When you join us, you will have:

  • Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
  • A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
  • Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions for our clients. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
  • World-class benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.

Your Impact

As a Data Science Specialist, you’ll collaborate in a dynamic, multi-disciplinary team of 3–6 experts, using your creativity and analytics expertise to drive client performance in areas like energy efficiency, product quality, and capacity. You’ll work side-by-side with client process engineers and analytics specialists, presenting your insights directly to executive audiences.

Internally, you’ll partner with our talented product engineers to develop innovative products and algorithms that address both current and future client needs. You’ll play a key role in codifying best practices, amplifying our impact both inside and outside the firm, and shaping the product roadmap.

You’ll be based in our Brussels office as part of McKinsey’s manufacturing AI solution, OptimusAI, which sits at the intersection of three practices: Global Energy and Materials, QuantumBlack, and Operations.

Global Energy and Materials: Advises production and investor companies across chemicals & agriculture, paper & packaging, metals & mining, oil & gas, and power generation on strategic, operational, and growth issues.

QuantumBlack, AI by McKinsey: The AI arm of our firm, helping clients transform faster, innovate smarter, and anticipate the future.

Operations: Assists clients in solving complex operational challenges by developing and implementing strategies and solutions for their most critical problems—connecting boardroom strategies to shop floor operators.

At the intersection of these groups, OptimusAI is a team of ~10 expert consultants, data scientists, and engineers focused on improving processing plant productivity through data analytics. We develop real-time AI advisory systems to support decision making in plant control rooms, optimize process control systems, and unlock plant bottlenecks through data-driven process changes.

OptimusAI also supports a global community of consultants by developing an innovative machine learning toolkit and codifying our approach to deliver significant and lasting client impact.

You’ll benefit from world-class training in business problem structuring and client advisory, working closely with senior manufacturing and analytics experts. Experience unparalleled career growth, with significant ownership and responsibility from day one, all within a collaborative, diverse, and non-hierarchical environment. Plus, seize the opportunity to travel to client sites—locally and around the world.

There is a flexibility to hire at different role level, depending on your experience.

Your Qualifications and Skills

  • Bachelor’s or master’s degree in data science, computer science, engineering, or a related field
  • 3+ years of hands-on experience in advanced analytics, ideally within heavy industry environments
  • Advanced Python programming skills, with a passion for writing clean, efficient, modular, and maintainable code
  • Proven ability to implement and maintain MLOps pipelines for seamless model integration and deployment
  • Strong DevOps and backend engineering capabilities, including experience with CI/CD pipelines, containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), and infrastructure as code (Terraform, Ansible)
  • Self-starter with excellent organizational abilities—able to independently initiate tasks and drive them to completion
  • Skilled in making rational, data-driven decisions based on facts and logical reasoning
  • Ability to create high-quality work products, such as PowerPoint presentations, Excel models, articles, or other written outputs
  • Exceptional time management skills to thrive in a complex, largely autonomous work environment
  • Willingness to work or attend meetings outside traditional business hours, and to take on projects at short notice when needed
  • Outstanding verbal and written communication skills in English with the ability to tailor your style to different audiences and seniority levels

Title: Senior Data Analyst (CX)
Description:
Department: CX Analytics

Employment Type: Full Time

Location: Remote

Reporting To: Maxim Manzyuk

Description

As a Customer Experience (CX) Data Analyst, you will play a key role in improving how we support our customers through data driven insights and operational analytics. You will work closely with CX, product, and operations teams to measure performance, identify improvement opportunities, and support better decision making across support workflows.

This role focuses on analytics, reporting, and process optimization, with exposure to automation and AI powered tools as part of everyday analytical work.

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Insights


Analyze large-scale datasets related to agent performance, support interactions and QA.
Write optimized SQL queries using tools like BigQuery and ClickHouse.
Build dashboards in Tableau, Metabase.
Detect behavioral and performance trends among agents and support teams.
Partner with QA to identify root causes in agent behavior and customer issues.



LLM & Prompting


Use LLM tools with practical prompt engineering for daily analytical tasks, summarization, and insights generation.
Ability to assess and profile LLM outputs for quality, relevance, and analytical correctness in business contexts.
Familiarity with LLM APIs and integrating model outputs into analytical or internal tooling workflows.



Automation & Workflow Optimization


Work with Airflow and Python to automate data tasks and support workflows.
Optimize analytics pipelines and data marts to improve performance, resource efficiency, and reliability.
Identify and refactor inefficient DAGs, queries, and transformations to ensure scalable and cost-effective data processing.
Suggest and co-build improvements in agent tooling, ticket routing, and SLA tracking.
Translate business inefficiencies into trackable metrics and measurable outcomes.



Cross-Functional Collaboration


Act as an analytical partner to Operations, Support Management, QA, and Training.
Work with Data Engineering to ensure clean, accurate, and well-modeled data pipelines.
Communicate findings clearly through presentations, visualizations, and concise documentation.

Work with Data Engineering to ensure clean, accurate, and well-modeled data pipelines.
Communicate findings clearly through presentations, visualizations, and concise documentation.



Job Benefits


We are an international Team of inspired professionals located all over the globe.
We have an inclusive company culture, embracing diversity, integrity and transparency. We strive for work-life balance and cherish the moments you spend with your loved ones, off-work. In the same spirit as for our product, we are caring and nurturing for our employees.
Our people are granted 100% trust and freedom to apply their own vision and come up with their ideas from day 1 at Tabby. You are the one who takes responsibility for your area of work. We encourage everyone to think and make decisions like Tabby was their own business, well because it is. Our employee stock options programme is available for everyone.
You will have an opportunity to learn and grow in one of the fastest growing fin tech companies in the region
We offer you relocation support as well as we guide you through all the process.

Title: Analytics Solution Officer - based in Luxembourg
Description:
This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence. The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support. The EIB, the European Union's bank, is seeking to recruit for its Group Information Systems Directorate (GIS), Data & Corporate Solutions Department (DCS), Data Management Division (DM), Analytics & Reporting Unit (AR) at its headquarters in Luxembourg, an Analytics Solution Officer*.

This is a full-time position at grade 5 for which the EIB offers a permanent contract.


internal benchmark Officer IT Applications


Panel interviews are anticipated for June/July 2026

Purpose

The Analytics Solution Officer ensures the stable, efficient and high‑quality delivery, evolution and governance of business‑critical IT applications and services supporting EIB Group business processes. The role exists to safeguard continuity of service, strengthen service delivery governance, and support the Bank’s ongoing modernisation initiatives by coordinating internal teams and external service providers in line with IT strategy, policies and contractual commitments.

The role focuses on the ownership and continuous evolution of analytics solutions, ensuring reliable operations while supporting ongoing change.

Operating Network

The role sits within the Analytics and Reporting Unit, which is responsible for the end‑to‑end lifecycle of analytics and reporting solutions supporting EIB Group business processes. This includes solution ownership, service delivery, vendor coordination and contribution to change initiatives.

The post holder works closely with business stakeholders, IT counterparts, senior management and external providers, and reports directly to the Head of Unit.

Accountabilities


Ensure end‑to‑end ownership and day‑to‑day delivery of analytics solutions and related services, maintaining availability, performance, security and compliance with internal standards.
Manage the full lifecycle of assigned solutions, from planning and sourcing through implementation, operation and controlled evolution, including roadmaps and service models.
Coordinate and steer external providers in a multi‑vendor setup, ensuring effective business‑as‑usual operations and measurable service performance through regular follow‑up, controls and reporting.
Support the definition and execution of externalisation initiatives, ensuring clear ownership, effective controls, service assurance and compliance with contractual obligations.
Liaise with business stakeholders, internal IT teams and external partners to translate functional needs into sustainable analytics solutions.
Contribute to project and delivery activities related to the acquisition of software and services, ensuring consistency with business priorities, enterprise architecture principles, IT security requirements and applicable policies.
Support continuous improvement of analytics platforms and services by identifying optimisation opportunities, promoting standardisation and reducing solution complexity where appropriate.
Maintain awareness of functional and technical evolutions of the platforms within the area of responsibility, advising stakeholders on relevant enhancements, options and implications.
Ensure appropriate governance, documentation, risk management and reporting across service delivery and change activities.
Support operational resilience through active involvement in incident handling, change coordination and issue escalation in collaboration with relevant parties.


Qualifications


University degree (minimum an equivalent to a Bachelor) preferably in Engineering, Information Technology, Information Management, Economics/Finance or a related field.
At least 5 years of relevant professional experience in analytics solution delivery, analytics platforms or reporting environments, including solution lifecycle or delivery coordination.
Proven experience working with external service providers in a multi‑vendor or outsourced environment.
Hands‑on experience with Power BI, including report development and platform administration (experience with Microsoft Fabric is an asset).
Exposure to data, reporting or analytics platforms and environments.
Experience with other business intelligence and analytics tools such as SAP BusinessObjects, SAP Analytics Cloud or Tableau is considered an asset.
Good understanding of application lifecycle management, IT service management principles and delivery governance.
Experience in coordinating projects involving multiple stakeholders and disciplines.
Experience in Data Warehouse design and methodologies would be an asset.
Ability to work effectively in a multicultural, international organization.
Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (*) with a good command of the other. (Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage).


Competencies

Find Out More About EIB Core Behavioural Skills Here

To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here

(*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.

We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity.

If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately.

By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation.

Deadline for applications: 21st May 2026

Title: Analytics Engineer (BI)
Description:
Company Description

Sports betting, gaming and interactive entertainment is changing, and we’re leading that change. By putting people first. By placing exciting, engaging and entertaining experiences at more fingertips than ever before. We're pulling into pole position by pushing boundaries further. With innovation. With technology. But most importantly, with people like you. Because when you join Entain, it’s your game. So let’s win together.

Job Description

This role sits at the core of our Data & BI function, translating data into actionable insights for stakeholders. The Associate Business Analyst will work closely with business teams to understand their needs, extract and analyse data and deliver clear, impactful outputs through dashboards, reporting, and ad-hoc analysis.

The position combines technical data skills with strong business understanding, requiring the ability not only to produce data, but to interpret it, challenge assumptions, and drive meaningful outcomes.

This is the ideal opportunity for someone with a passion for sports to develop their analytical and reporting skills within a fast-paced and competitive environment.


Extract, manipulate, and analyse large datasets from Snowflake using SQL, turning data into clear, actionable insights - not just outputs.
Design, build, and maintain dbt models for the analytics semantic layer, ensuring data consistency, documentation, and reusability.
Build and maintain dashboards and reporting solutions, ensuring they are scalable, reliable, and widely adopted by the business.
Translate business questions into structured analysis, delivering concise and impactful insights to stakeholders.
Deliver ad-hoc analysis to support decision-making across multiple business areas, with a strong focus on speed and accuracy.
Contribute to the development and maintenance of self-serve analytics, enabling teams to independently access and explore data.
Apply version control best practices (Git) to all analytical work, including dbt models and documentation.
Support team members in dbt, Git, and data modelling practices through day-to-day collaboration and knowledge sharing.
Work closely with Data Engineering and other teams to improve data availability, quality, and usability.
Continuously challenge assumptions, identify opportunities, and proactively propose data-driven solutions to business problems.


Qualifications

Essential:


A degree in Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or related field.
Demonstrable experience with SQL and data analysis.
Advanced SQL skills, with the ability to query, optimise, and model large datasets in Snowflake or similar cloud data warehouses.
Hands-on experience with dbt (building, testing, and documenting models).
Working knowledge of Git and version control workflows.
Understanding of data modelling principles (star schema, dimensional modelling).
Proficiency in Excel. Fluent level of written and spoken English.


Desired


Experience with BI and data visualisation tools (e.g. Omni, Looker, Power BI, Tableau).
Interest in sports and/or familiarity with the sports betting / iGaming industry.
An understanding of statistical analysis techniques.
Experience working in digital, data-driven, or iGaming environments.
Experience with tools such as Google Analytics, Optimove, or similar marketing/data platforms
Portuguese and/or Spanish


Additional Information

At Entain, we know that signing top players requires a great starting package, and plenty of support to inspire peak performance. Join us, and a competitive salary is just the beginning.

Depending on your role and location, you can expect to receive benefits like


A regular bonus
Healthcare support
A stake in our success through our ShareSave scheme
Great development opportunities
Wellbeing support, and so much more.


And outside of this, you’ll have the chance to turn recognition from leaders and colleagues into amazing prizes, join a winning team of talented people and be a part of an inclusive and supporting community where everyone is celebrated for being themselves.

Should you need any adjustments or accommodations to the recruitment process, at either application or interview, please contact us.

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
About Donorbox

Donorbox is a leading fundraising platform and donor management system for nonprofit organizations. Our mission is to accelerate positive impact worldwide by helping nonprofits become highly effective at raising funds and managing their supporter base. Since 2014, we have powered more than 100,000 global organizations to close to $3B in donations. 🚀

Our fast-growing company is profitable and bootstrapped with a healthy run rate. We have a fully distributed and diverse 150-person team based in 16+ states and 23+ countries.

🏅Donorbox is rated the #1 software for fundraising, donor management, and nonprofit payment on G2 based on hundreds of verified customer reviews. We're proud of what we do, and our product reflects it!

The Role

Donorbox is seeking a Product Analyst to join our growing team. Reporting directly to the Group Product Manager, the person will be a key player in helping our product teams make data-driven decisions. The role's primary focus will be on defining, implementing, and analyzing product metrics such as in-app events and conversion funnels. The person will also be responsible for ensuring the integrity and accuracy of our data collection, acting as a crucial link between our Product and Data departments. They will work closely with product managers and designers to provide the insights they need to build and iterate on successful features, and track the key results for each objective of every product team.

Responsibilities


Partner with Product Managers to define, measure and track North Star metrics and leading indicators for every initiative tackled, connecting them to the overall OKRs
Work with product managers to establish, implement, and analyze product conversion funnels.
Collaborate with engineering teams to ensure accurate and complete data collection for all new product launches and feature updates.
Serve as the primary point of contact for product usage data requests from the design and product teams, providing them with the necessary insights to inform their decisions.
Bridge the communication and technical gap between the Product and Data departments, translating business needs into data requirements.
Monitor data quality and integrity, troubleshooting and resolving any issues related to event tracking or data loss.
Assist in setting up and analyzing A/B tests and other experimental feature rollouts.
Prepare and present regular reports and dashboards on product performance to stakeholders.


Qualifications & Experience


Minimum 5 years of experience as a Product Analyst or Product Owner with a strong analytical focus in a SaaS environment
Proven hands-on experience with advanced product analytics tools like Rudderstack, Segment or Userpilot including successful implementation and ongoing use.
Deep expertise in web analytics platforms such as Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and Google Tag Manager (GTM).
Proven track-record with A/B testing and feature rollout initiatives
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a keen eye for detail.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across different teams.
Proficiency in data visualization tools (e.g., Looker Studio, Tableau, Power BI).
Experience with SQL for data querying and analysis.


Bonus


Familiarity with data warehousing concepts and technologies.
Experience in a high-growth or startup environment.
Knowledge of scripting languages like Python or R for data analysis.
Background in user experience (UX) or product design.
Familiarity with agile development methodologies.


Details

Salary: $50k-$60k USD, depending on experience and location


Benefits & Perks


Fully remote work from the comfort of your home
Eligibility for employee equity plan (stock options)

Generous time off policy of 21 days (birthday included 🎉), 8 holidays of your choice, and 2 paid volunteer days
Wellness program with fitness and mindfulness classes
Love your work and our mission of serving nonprofits!

Title: Business Support Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Inetum is a European leader in digital services. Inetum’s team of 28,000 consultants and specialists strive every day to make a digital impact for businesses, public sector entities and society. Inetum’s solutions aim at contributing to its clients’ performance and innovation as well as the common good.

Present in 19 countries with a dense network of sites, Inetum partners with major software publishers to meet the challenges of digital transformation with proximity and flexibility.

Driven by its ambition for growth and scale, Inetum generated sales of 2.5 billion euros in 2023.

Job Description

This open position encompasses activities related to the management of user access and Business Support for a client applications


Test requirements for new business implementations
Identify automation opportunities
Participation and management of projects related to business application needs
Identifying technical errors to improve performance of remote business applications
Assist in software installations and configurations
Participate in workshops and follow-ups regarding business activities
Asset Management Control
Review user identities and work with applications Business Owner, Technical Owner and Access management to perform any required corrective / preventive actions
Perform user account management tasks, such as creating, modifying, and deactivating user accounts
Monitor and analyse IAM-related logs and reports to identify potential security issues or unauthorized access attempts
Assign and revoke access rights as needed
Ensure compliance with security standards and regulations
Link external IT helpdesk support to local business needs
Monitor and follow-up the activity performed by external IT Helpdesk service"
Assist in software installations and configurations
Review user identities and work with applications Business Owner, Technical Owner and Access management to perform any required corrective / preventive actions
Monitor and follow-up the activity performed by external IT Helpdesk service
Participation and management of projects related to business application needs


Qualifications

Technical Skills:


Minimum 1 year of experience in similar activities
ServiceNow
Active Directory
Systematic, autonomous and logical thinking
Microsoft Office 365


Language Skills


English – Expert
French – Notions


Soft Skills


Ability to work independently and in a team environment. Work in a team and communicate effectively, being a good listener, reliable.
Good listening and questioning ability.
Ability to plan work ahead and to prioritize workload.
Respect the procedures, methodologies and directives.
Good learning skills.


Other Details

Ability to travel within Portugal

Title: Spontaneous Application
Description:
🌍 About Keyrus

Keyrus is a global consulting and technology company focused on making data matter — truly matter — from a human perspective.

Founded in 1996, Keyrus operates in 28+ countries across 5 continents, with more than 3,300 people worldwide. Our strength comes from combining deep expertise in Data & Analytics, AI, Digital, and Management Consulting with a strong understanding of business realities.

Data is never the goal in itself.

We use data to shape understanding, design meaningful experiences, and enable better, real-life decisions.

At Keyrus, we also believe companies have a responsibility beyond performance. Through our Foundation and ESG initiatives, we actively contribute to sustainability, inclusion, and positive societal impact.

#OneTeamOneKeyrus

🎯 This is a Spontaneous Application

You will work at the intersection of data, technology, and business, helping our clients turn complexity into clarity and insights into impact.

You will collaborate with multidisciplinary teams in an international environment and contribute to projects that drive performance, innovation, and sustainable growth.

Note: All applications (CVs) must be submitted in English.

💥 Your Impact

In This Role, You Will


Contribute to delivering high-quality solutions that solve real business problems
Translate data and technical concepts into clear, actionable outcomes
Collaborate with consultants, engineers, and stakeholders across teams and geographies
Support clients in their data and digital transformation journeys
Continuously improve how data is used to inform decisions and create value


⚡ The challenges you will find at Keyrus


You will work in complex, evolving client environments
You are expected to balance delivery quality with pragmatism
You will collaborate with diverse profiles across functions and cultures
Autonomy increases with seniority — so does responsibility


👤 What We Value In Our Consultants

Must-haves


Proven experience in Data Analytics, AI and/or Data Engineering tools
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
A collaborative mindset and sense of ownership
Fluency in English
Must be a Portuguese or European citizen, or hold a valid work permit for Portugal


Nice-to-haves


Experience in consulting environments
Exposure to international or multicultural projects
Knowledge in other languages


💰 How Our Salary Ranges Work

At Keyrus, salary ranges reflect different levels of mastery and impact within the same role — not different job titles.


Bottom of the range You meet the core requirements and will need ramp-up time and support.
Middle of the range You are fully autonomous from Day 1 and deliver consistently.
Top of the range You are a reference for the role, mentor others, and raise the bar for the team.


Final offers are based on experience, autonomy, scope, and market context, and are discussed transparently during the process.

🎁 What We Offer At Keyrus Portugal


Competitive salary aligned with your experience and the data market
Meal allowance: €10.20/day
Flexible benefits plan
Private medical insurance
22 days of annual leave, increasing every 3 years (up to 25 days)
Continuous learning via KLX – Keyrus Learning Experience
A collaborative, international, and human-centred work environment


💙 Why Keyrus?

Joining Keyrus Means Joining


A market leader in Data Intelligence
A company where people, trust, and diversity are core values
An environment that values ownership, flexibility, and innovation
A place where different backgrounds and perspectives are not just welcomed = they are essential


We believe diversity drives better thinking, stronger teams, and better outcomes.

Everyone belongs at Keyrus.

♿ Equal Opportunity Statement

We are committed to building an inclusive workplace and encourage applications from all backgrounds, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
A Job? Or a Lifetime Experience? Start Yours Here!


A nossa missão é ser uma parte significativa da carreira das nossas pessoas. À medida que crescemos, cresce também a determinação em oferecer a melhor experiência aos nossos colaboradores e clientes e é exatamente isso que nos move. Somos uma empresa portuguesa de consultoria tecnológica com escritórios em Lisboa, Porto e Óbidos e representações no Brasil e Tunísia. Contamos com mais de 11 anos de expertise no mercado, e hoje somos um universo de cerca de 400 pessoas a trabalhar em contexto presencial, remoto ou híbrido em projetos em mais de 20 países.


Acreditamos que grandes pessoas fazem empresas de sucesso e por isso mesmo defendemos a valorização, reconhecimento e crescimento dos nossos profissionais.


Não queremos ser mais uma linha do teu CV, queremos que connosco vivas uma Lifetime Experience. Apostamos em formações e certificações que confirmam a tua real progressão, na promoção de um saudável work-life balance e na oferta de benefícios com impacto na tua vida pessoal e carreira.


O que procuramos


  • Licenciatura em Economia, Estatística, Matemática, Gestão ou similar;
  • Mais de 3 anos de experiência profissional em funções como Data Analyst;
  • Conhecimentos sólidos de SQL Server;
  • Conhecimentos sólidos de PowerBI (dashboards, DAX, modelagem de dados);
  • Conhecimentos sólidos de SSIS
  • Desenvolver, executar e automatizar processos ETL
  • Bons conhecimentos de inglês (B2 ou superior).


Ser Affinity


  • #Affinidade – Criares relações de proximidade e empatia com colegas, clientes e candidatos (e não apenas com tecnologias, linguagens e plataformas). Espírito de equipa e boa disposição;
  • #Ambição – Quereres fazer mais e melhor. Vontade de exceder expectativas e evoluir pessoal e profissionalmente;
  • #Ação – Energia para fazer acontecer. Mostrares proatividade e capacidade de iniciativa;
  • #Aprendizagem – Vontade de evoluir individual e coletivamente e tornares-te um expert no mercado tecnológico.


A tua #LifetimeExperience


  • Participar em projetos nacionais e internacionais numa empresa baseada em relacionamentos pessoais, na simplicidade e na eficiência, com uma ação disruptiva no mercado tecnológico;
  • Aceder a um plano de carreira e formação personalizado e diretamente proporcional ao teu desempenho e interesse;
  • Fazer parte de um ambiente acolhedor, de confiança, respeito e informalidade;
  • Integrar as nossas Affinity Communities (de desporto, tecnologia, hobbies, etc.) e participar ativamente em projetos de responsabilidade social e ambiental;
  • Tirar partido de um conjunto de benefícios, parcerias, descontos eventos e dinâmicas internas.


Para além da tua satisfação profissional queremos proporcionar-te oportunidades de lazer e convívio memoráveis e dignos de uma experiência Affinity.


Join the Experience!


Envia-nos a tua candidatura e aproveita para nos conhecer e ir acompanhando em:

https://www.instagram.com/affinityportugal/

https://www.linkedin.com/company/2701281/admin/

  • https://twitter.com/WithAffinity

Title: Data Analyst Trainee
Description:
About The Job


The Transaction Banking business concerns two fundamental business lines:
Cash Management: all products that assist our customers with their liquidity needs (current accounts, term deposits, payments & collections, forex)
Global Trade Solutions: all products that assist our customers with their global trade needs (letters of credit, guarantees, supply chain management, factoring)
As Transaction Banking Chief Operating Officer (TB COO) team, we assist our different business team in multiple topics, both on daily/monthly needs as in project mode. Within the COO team we have a small team of data scientists focusing on data, who are involved in the data use cases from exploration phase up to production delivery.
This position provides an excellent opportunity to learn about the overall banking industry as a whole, more specifically dealing with the Transaction Banking business across all regions (EMEA, APAC, AMER)
The Data Analyst Trainee’s main mission is to provide support to the Cash Management fee analysis Unit and to contribute to the pricing to billing initiatives

Your Main Activities Are


Automate business dashboards as prototypes and prepare them to be put in production, working closely with the team´s data analysts
Report to Top line TB Management and TB COO on all data queries and data needs
Contribute to the development and governance of our data and support the different Cash Management business line with data-driven decision-making
Meet different stakeholders to frame their data needs, based on your findings collect the necessary data, process the data, and turn it into meaningful tools meeting the requirements expressed by the teams
Participate and interact with inter-disciplinary teams such as IT, Business and Operations and contribute to the improvement and streamlining of data systems within the company to drive innovation


Profile And Skills To Success


Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Management, Business Intelligence, Business, Economics or Mathematics
Fluency in English
Highly proficient with MS Excel
Ability to quickly comprehend new software tools and learn how to use them optimally
Great attention to detail
Excellent interpersonal, communication and influence skills
Good conceptualization skills and data exposure
Open, curious and eager to learn and discover day to day banking products


Why joining BNP Paribas?


Leading banking institution


Leading player in banking and financial services in Europe, BNP Paribas operates in 64 countries and has nearly 178,000 employees, including more than 144,000 in Europe


International reach


The Group holds key positions in several areas of banking and financial services that are structured around three operating divisions:


Corporate & Institutional Banking (CIB) connects companies needing financing and institutional clients looking for investment opportunities division that offers tailored financial solutions for corporate and institutional clients.
Commercial, Personal Banking & Services (CPBS) brings together all our commercial banks and several specialised businesses
Investment & Protection Services (IPS) combines a unique continuum of products and services across protection, saving, investment and real estate including specialised businesses offering a wide range of savings, investment and protection services
Our presence in Portugal


In Portugal since 1985, BNP Paribas today has more than 9.220 employees, distributed across the Group's 10 business entities established in the country. Its presence also extends to 11 centres of expertise providing value-added services to various countries where the BNP Paribas Group also operates.


Diversity and Inclusion commitment


BNP Paribas ´ambition Is To Cultivate An Open And Responsive Environment For All That Encourages Collaboration And Interaction. We Demonstrate Our Commitment By Actively Contributing To The Group’s Diversity And Inclusion Strategy And Goals, In Line With The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. As Part Of Our Local Corporate And Social Responsibility – Diversity & Inclusion Strategy, BNP Paribas Portugal Is Committed To


Promote a fair and unbiased recruitment process and offer professional development opportunities to all employees
Celebrate diversity and advocate for inclusion, both externally and internally, encouraging employee’s participation, creating space for different voices to be heard.


To foster the effort of BNP Paribas Portugal, multiple initiatives and events take place throughout the year where our people can find out more on the importance of diversity inclusion at the workplace and in our society.


Remote Working Conditions


At BNP Paribas, we embrace a Smart Working framework based on trust, autonomy and collaboration. Within this framework, eligible employees can benefit from flexible remote working options adapted to our hybrid working environment. To ensure a comfortable and efficient working setup, eligible employees are provided with both office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional items at reduced prices.


Commitment towards work/life balance


Supporting employees in a hybrid way of working while providing them with the means to maintain a work-life balance is an essential dimension of our Smart Working program. At BNP Paribas, we care about our employees’ wellbeing and promote a culture of good integration between work and personal life.

To find out more on why you should join BNP Paribas please read our Employee Value Proposition and our Career path page.


Please note that only applications submitted in English will be considered.
In case you are selected for this role, further documentation will be requested to support your hiring process.

  • Ability to quickly comprehend new software tools and learn how to use them optimally
  • Great attention to detail
  • Excellent interpersonal, communication and influence skills
  • Good conceptualization skills and data exposure
  • Open, curious and eager to learn and discover day to day banking products

Why joining BNP Paribas?

  • Leading banking institution

Leading player in banking and financial services in Europe, BNP Paribas operates in 64 countries and has nearly 178,000 employees, including more than 144,000 in Europe

  • International reach

The Group holds key positions in several areas of banking and financial services that are structured around three operating divisions:

  • Corporate & Institutional Banking (CIB) connects companies needing financing and institutional clients looking for investment opportunities division that offers tailored financial solutions for corporate and institutional clients.
  • Commercial, Personal Banking & Services (CPBS) brings together all our commercial banks and several specialised businesses
  • Investment & Protection Services (IPS) combines a unique continuum of products and services across protection, saving, investment and real estate including specialised businesses offering a wide range of savings, investment and protection services
  • Our presence in Portugal

In Portugal since 1985, BNP Paribas today has more than 9.220 employees, distributed across the Group's 10 business entities established in the country. Its presence also extends to 11 centres of expertise providing value-added services to various countries where the BNP Paribas Group also operates.

  • Diversity and Inclusion commitment

BNP Paribas ´ambition Is To Cultivate An Open And Responsive Environment For All That Encourages Collaboration And Interaction. We Demonstrate Our Commitment By Actively Contributing To The Group’s Diversity And Inclusion Strategy And Goals, In Line With The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. As Part Of Our Local Corporate And Social Responsibility – Diversity & Inclusion Strategy, BNP Paribas Portugal Is Committed To

  • Promote a fair and unbiased recruitment process and offer professional development opportunities to all employees
  • Celebrate diversity and advocate for inclusion, both externally and internally, encouraging employee’s participation, creating space for different voices to be heard.

To foster the effort of BNP Paribas Portugal, multiple initiatives and events take place throughout the year where our people can find out more on the importance of diversity inclusion at the workplace and in our society.

  • Remote Working Conditions

At BNP Paribas, we embrace a Smart Working framework based on trust, autonomy and collaboration. Within this framework, eligible employees can benefit from flexible remote working options adapted to our hybrid working environment. To ensure a comfortable and efficient working setup, eligible employees are provided with both office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional items at reduced prices.

  • Commitment towards work/life balance

Supporting employees in a hybrid way of working while providing them with the means to maintain a work-life balance is an essential dimension of our Smart Working program. At BNP Paribas, we care about our employees’ wellbeing and promote a culture of good integration between work and personal life.

To find out more on why you should join BNP Paribas please read our Employee Value Proposition and our Career path page.

  • Please note that only applications submitted in English will be considered.
  • In case you are selected for this role, further documentation will be requested to support your hiring process.

Title: Administrativo de Backoffice - Clínica Lusíadas Oriente
HTML:
O Grupo Lusíadas Saúde, com 25 anos de história, é hoje uma das referências na saúde em Portugal.

Crescemos com base na proximidade, na inovação e numa forma de cuidar que valoriza as pessoas — os nossos profissionais, os nossos clientes e todas as diferenças que nos tornam únicos. Valorizamos a abertura, compaixão, lealdade e integridade, entendendo que é o que torna a nossa equipa verdadeiramente única.

Somos uma equipa de 8.000 profissionais para quem “Sabemos Cuidar” é um compromisso diário.

Neste momento, pretendemos recrutar um(a) Administrativo(a) de Backoffice para a nossa Clínica Lusíadas Oriente.

Perfil pretendido:

  • Habilitações académicas ao nível do 12º ano de escolaridade (obrigatório);
  • Valorizamos experiência profissional na área de receção na saúde;
  • Conhecimentos da língua inglesa a nível escrito e oral;
  • Disponibilidade para turnos rotativos



Principais Responsabilidades:

  • Apoio ao cliente, demonstrando empatia e disponibilidade;
  • Apoio à equipa de front office;
  • Agendamento de exames e outros procedimentos;
  • Organização e otimização de marcações;
  • Realizar funções de carácter administrativo e expediente geral.



Benefícios de Ser Lusíadas

  • Dia de aniversário oferecido;
  • Seguro de saúde para o próprio;
  • Formação contínua e mobilidade interna – Academia Lusíadas | Knowledge Center;
  • Your Baby Matters | Kit maternidade;
  • Apoios ao regresso às aulas e Natal;
  • Clube Lusíadas Vantagens | Descontos exclusivos;
  • Seguro para a família e condições especiais para agregados familiares;
  • Apoio a filhos de colaboradores com necessidades especiais – Lusíadas em Família.



No Grupo Lusíadas Saúde promovemos um ambiente inclusivo e diverso, onde valorizamos as diferenças e garantimos igualdade de oportunidades para todas as pessoas, independentemente da sua identidade, origem ou condição.

Se partilha os nossos valores e quer integrar um projeto de referência na saúde em Portugal, envie o seu CV atualizado e junte-se a uma equipa que sabe cuidar!

Title: Data Reporting Analyst
Description:
Procuras dar um passo na tua carreira nas áreas de IT e Telecomunicações?

Então chegaste ao sítio certo – à Smart!

A tecnologia, a transformação e a inovação fazem parte do nosso ADN. Somos uma empresa de consultoria tecnológica com mais de uma década de experiência no desenvolvimento de projetos nacionais e internacionais em IT e Telecomunicações e queremos fazer parte da tua história.




O que precisas para ser um Analista de Dados Reporting e Transformação?

Domínio de ferramentas Microsoft Office (preferencialmente Access e Excel)
Experiência em funções de análise funcional / BI
Sólidos conhecimentos de SQL
Conhecimento das especificações, funcionamento e requisitos técnicos dos sistemas de xTM




Quais serão as tuas funções na SMART?

Acompanhar e monitorizar a performance de operações, de forma a assegurar a qualidade do serviço prestado end-to-end.
Recolher e sistematizar dados operacionais das diferentes áreas da DGR, elaborando relatórios para diferentes intervenientes
Desenvolver e implementar procedimentos para auditorias aleatórias e regulares das intervenções no terreno, incluindo atividades como a preparação de checklists de auditoria, a programação de auditorias e a análise inicial dos resultados
Identificar os equipamentos xTM que não cumprem os requisitos mínimos de rede
Auxiliar na análise das funcionalidades críticas dos sistemas de suporte à operação, contribuindo com insights para garantir a escalabilidade e flexibilidade
Identificar, criar e acompanhar as evoluções (EVs) junto das equipas de desenvolvimento




Porquê ser SMART?

Tornamos reais os projetos em que participamos.
Amamos o que fazemos e temos orgulho no resultado do nosso trabalho.
Somos simples e eficientes.
Valorizamos as nossas pessoas.
Somos uma equipa dinâmica, íntegra e de confiança.




Onde podes ser SMART?

Title: Business Data Analyst
HTML:
Company Description

Inetum is a European leader in digital services. Inetum’s team of 27,000 consultants and specialists strive every day to make a digital impact for businesses, public sector entities and society. Inetum’s solutions aim at contributing to its clients’ performance and innovation as well as the common good. Present in 19 countries with a dense network of sites, Inetum partners with major software publishers to meet the challenges of digital transformation with proximity and flexibility. Driven by its ambition for growth and scale, Inetum generated sales of 2.4 billion euros in 2024.

Job Description

Are you passionate about turning data into actionable insights? At Inetum, we are looking for a Business Data Analyst to bridge the gap between data and business, ensuring that information is available to support strategic decisions efficiently.

Key Responsibilities

  • Gather and define business requirements for data initiatives;
  • Extract and analyze data from commercial and analytical systems for ad hoc analysis;
  • Design and structure data models to create valuable products and ensure data consistency;
  • Perform business analysis to support operations and decision-making;
  • Communicate effectively with stakeholders and translate insights into actionable recommendations;
  • Contribute to the evolution of our Data Warehouse and Datahub.

Deliverables

  • Development of dashboards and data visualization strategies;
  • Creation of documents and reports to support decision-making;
  • Compilation of information and preparation of analytical reports;
  • User Stories for Data Warehouse enhancements;
  • Data modeling within the Data Warehouse;
  • Ad hoc data collection from commercial and analytical systems.

Qualifications

  • Up to 10 years of experience in similar roles;
  • Advanced knowledge of SQL for data extraction and manipulation;
  • Strong experience with dashboards and data visualization tools;
  • Advanced Excel and proficiency in Office tools;
  • Preferably experience with SAS Enterprise Guide and DOMO;
  • Solid understanding of data modeling and warehousing concepts.

Title: Educador de primeira infância
HTML:
A Creche na Quinta é uma instituição privada dedicada a promover o desenvolvimento integral de crianças em idade pré-escolar. Damos às crianças o espaço, a liberdade e a orientação que necessitam para explorarem o seu próprio mundo.

Procuramos um(a) Educador(a) de Infância dedicado(a), criativo(a) e colaborativo(a) para integrar a nossa equipa.


Funções:

  • Planear e dinamizar atividades pedagógicas adequadas às idades;
  • Acompanhamento do desenvolvimento cognitivo, motor, emocional e social das crianças;
  • Supervisão de rotinas diárias (higiene, alimentação, sono) e promoção de hábitos de autonomia;
  • Registar observações e comunicar o progresso às famílias;
  • Colaborar com auxiliares, psicólogos/educadores de apoio e restante equipa;
  • Garantir um ambiente seguro, inclusivo e estimulante.

Requisitos obrigatórios:

  • Licenciatura/Mestrado em Educação de Infância (ou equivalência legal);
  • Excelente comunicação, organização e trabalho em equipa;
  • Ética de trabalho (responsabilidade, integridade, humildade, empenho e compromisso);
  • Fluência em português;

Requisitos desejáveis:

  • Formação em primeiros socorros, metodologias ativas, inglês básico, entre outros;
  • Paixões e competências em áreas como música, artes, literacia;
  • Fluência em inglês;

Condições:

  • Local: Ameixoeira/Alta de Lisboa
  • Contrato: Contrato de Trabalho sem Termo
  • Benefícios: subsídio de refeição, formação contínua, apoio em desenvolvimento profissional,

Tipo de oferta: Período Integral, Contrato de trabalho sem termo.

Habilitações literárias:

  • Mestrado (Preferencial), possível finalista ou recém-graduado.

Idioma:

  • Português (Obrigatório)

Title: Data Visualization
Description:
A Job? Or a Lifetime Experience? Start Yours Here!




A nossa missão é ser uma parte significativa da carreira das nossas pessoas. À medida que crescemos, cresce também a determinação em oferecer a melhor experiência aos nossos colaboradores e clientes e é exatamente isso que nos move. Somos uma empresa portuguesa de consultoria tecnológica com escritórios em Lisboa, Porto e Óbidos e representações no Brasil e Tunísia. Contamos com mais de 13 anos de expertise no mercado, e hoje somos um universo de cerca de 400 pessoas a trabalhar em contexto presencial, remoto ou híbrido em projetos em +20 países.




Acreditamos que grandes pessoas fazem empresas de sucesso e por isso mesmo defendemos a valorização, reconhecimento e crescimento dos nossos profissionais.




Não queremos ser mais uma linha do teu CV, queremos que connosco vivas uma Lifetime Experience. Apostamos em formações e certificações que confirmam a tua real progressão, na promoção de um saudável work-life balance e na oferta de benefícios com impacto na tua vida pessoal e carreira.




#welcometoyourfuture




O que procuramos:

Experiência em Business Intelligence, com foco especializado em Visualização de Dados e processos de ETL.
Domínio avançado de ferramentas de visualização líderes de mercado, como SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), Tableau, Power BI ou Qlik.
Experiência prática em ferramentas de Data Prep, tais como Tableau, Power Query ou Alteryx, para limpeza, transformação e normalização de dados.
Sólidos conhecimentos de SQL e experiência na manipulação de bases de dados e diversos formatos de ficheiros (CSV, JSON, XML).
Capacidade de integração de dados, garantindo a ligação eficiente entre ferramentas de BI e fontes variadas como APIs, Data Lakes e bases de dados relacionais.
Forte competência analítica, assegurando a integridade, qualidade e precisão dos dados em todo o ciclo de visualização.
Excelentes competências de comunicação, com capacidade de colaborar com stakeholders para traduzir requisitos de negócio em dashboards intuitivos e acionáveis.
Domínio do Inglês (mínimo B2




Ser Affinity

#Affinidade – Criares relações de proximidade e empatia com colegas, clientes e candidatos (e não apenas com tecnologias, linguagens e plataformas). Espírito de equipa e boa disposição;

#Ambição – Quereres fazer mais e melhor. Vontade de exceder expectativas e evoluir pessoal e profissionalmente;

#Ação – Energia para fazer acontecer. Mostrares proatividade e capacidade de iniciativa;

#Aprendizagem – Vontade de evoluir individual e coletivamente e tornares-te um expert no mercado tecnológico.

#Assertividade – Comunicação transparente e honesta, sempre com feedback construtivo.




A tua #LifetimeExperience

Participar em projetos nacionais e internacionais numa empresa baseada em relacionamentos pessoais, na simplicidade e na eficiência, com uma ação disruptiva no mercado tecnológico;
Aceder a um plano de carreira e formação personalizado e diretamente proporcional ao teu desempenho e interesse;
Fazer parte de um ambiente acolhedor, de confiança, respeito e informalidade;
Integrar as nossas Affinity Communities (de desporto, tecnologia, hobbies, etc.) e participar ativamente em projetos de responsabilidade social e ambiental;
Tirar partido de um conjunto de benefícios, parcerias, descontos eventos e dinâmicas internas.




Para além da tua satisfação profissional queremos proporcionar-te oportunidades de lazer e convívio memoráveis e dignos de uma experiência Affinity.




Join the Experience!

HTML:
A Job? Or a Lifetime Experience? Start Yours Here!


A nossa missão é ser uma parte significativa da carreira das nossas pessoas. À medida que crescemos, cresce também a determinação em oferecer a melhor experiência aos nossos colaboradores e clientes e é exatamente isso que nos move. Somos uma empresa portuguesa de consultoria tecnológica com escritórios em Lisboa, Porto e Óbidos e representações no Brasil e Tunísia. Contamos com mais de 13 anos de expertise no mercado, e hoje somos um universo de cerca de 400 pessoas a trabalhar em contexto presencial, remoto ou híbrido em projetos em +20 países.


Acreditamos que grandes pessoas fazem empresas de sucesso e por isso mesmo defendemos a valorização, reconhecimento e crescimento dos nossos profissionais.


Não queremos ser mais uma linha do teu CV, queremos que connosco vivas uma Lifetime Experience. Apostamos em formações e certificações que confirmam a tua real progressão, na promoção de um saudável work-life balance e na oferta de benefícios com impacto na tua vida pessoal e carreira.


#welcometoyourfuture


O que procuramos:

  • Experiência em Business Intelligence, com foco especializado em Visualização de Dados e processos de ETL.
  • Domínio avançado de ferramentas de visualização líderes de mercado, como SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), Tableau, Power BI ou Qlik.
  • Experiência prática em ferramentas de Data Prep, tais como Tableau, Power Query ou Alteryx, para limpeza, transformação e normalização de dados.
  • Sólidos conhecimentos de SQL e experiência na manipulação de bases de dados e diversos formatos de ficheiros (CSV, JSON, XML).
  • Capacidade de integração de dados, garantindo a ligação eficiente entre ferramentas de BI e fontes variadas como APIs, Data Lakes e bases de dados relacionais.
  • Forte competência analítica, assegurando a integridade, qualidade e precisão dos dados em todo o ciclo de visualização.
  • Excelentes competências de comunicação, com capacidade de colaborar com stakeholders para traduzir requisitos de negócio em dashboards intuitivos e acionáveis.
  • Domínio do Inglês (mínimo B2


Ser Affinity

#Affinidade – Criares relações de proximidade e empatia com colegas, clientes e candidatos (e não apenas com tecnologias, linguagens e plataformas). Espírito de equipa e boa disposição;

#Ambição – Quereres fazer mais e melhor. Vontade de exceder expectativas e evoluir pessoal e profissionalmente;

#Ação – Energia para fazer acontecer. Mostrares proatividade e capacidade de iniciativa;

#Aprendizagem – Vontade de evoluir individual e coletivamente e tornares-te um expert no mercado tecnológico.

#Assertividade – Comunicação transparente e honesta, sempre com feedback construtivo.


A tua #LifetimeExperience

  • Participar em projetos nacionais e internacionais numa empresa baseada em relacionamentos pessoais, na simplicidade e na eficiência, com uma ação disruptiva no mercado tecnológico;
  • Aceder a um plano de carreira e formação personalizado e diretamente proporcional ao teu desempenho e interesse;
  • Fazer parte de um ambiente acolhedor, de confiança, respeito e informalidade;
  • Integrar as nossas Affinity Communities (de desporto, tecnologia, hobbies, etc.) e participar ativamente em projetos de responsabilidade social e ambiental;
  • Tirar partido de um conjunto de benefícios, parcerias, descontos eventos e dinâmicas internas.


Para além da tua satisfação profissional queremos proporcionar-te oportunidades de lazer e convívio memoráveis e dignos de uma experiência Affinity.


Join the Experience!


Envia-nos a tua candidatura e aproveita para nos conhecer e ir acompanhando em:

https://www.instagram.com/affinityportugal/

https://www.linkedin.com/company/2701281/admin/

https://twitter.com/WithAffinity

Title: Balcão - Assistente de Sala (M/F) - El Corte Inglês
HTML:
Fundada em 1998, a Plateform é um grupo de restauração com ADN 100% português. No seuportfólio de restaurantes constam 28 marcas e um universo de cerca de 150 restaurantes. Procuramos proporcionar experiências únicas! Desde a descoberta de vários universos gastronómicos a momentos memoráveis que podes oferecer aos nossos clientes.

Se tens paixão pela área da restauração, temos a oferta perfeita para ti!

O que esperamos de ti?

  • Receber e acompanhar o cliente de forma atenciosa e profissional;
  • Apresentar o menu, esclarecer dúvidas e fazer sugestões adequadas;
  • Realizar o levantamento e entrega de pedidos de forma correta;
  • Assegurar a limpeza e organização da sala, antes, durante e após o serviço;
  • Garantir que todos os serviços prestados ao cliente estão em conformidade com os standards operacionais;
  • Colaborar com a equipa de forma a manter um serviço eficiente;
  • Cumprir os requisitos de HACCP.


No fundo…

A tua MISSÃO passará por atender e acolher os nossos clientes, proporcionando uma experiência gastronómica excecional!

O que procuramos?

  • Gosto pelo atendimento ao cliente e atenção ao detalhe;
  • Energia, boa disposição e espírito de equipa;
  • Proatividade;
  • Boas competências de comunicação;
  • Fluência na língua inglesa;
  • Vontade de crescer.


O que oferecemos?

  • Integração num grupo sólido, dinâmico e em expansão;
  • Salário flexível (possibilidade de adiantar parte do teu salário);
  • Possibilidade de ter mais 3 dias de férias;
  • Refeição gratuita no local de trabalho;
  • Dia de aniversário oferecido pela empresa;
  • Integração numa equipa jovem e dinâmica;
  • Possibilidade de progressão de carreira;
  • Part-time com horários fixos;
  • Full-time com horários rotativos;
  • Horários seguidos com direito a duas folgas semanais (seguidas);


És o ingrediente perfeito?

Candidata-te!

Title: Rececionista - Clínica Lusíadas Oriente
HTML:
O Grupo Lusíadas Saúde, com 25 anos de história, é hoje uma das referências na saúde em Portugal.

Crescemos com base na proximidade, na inovação e numa forma de cuidar que valoriza as pessoas — os nossos profissionais, os nossos clientes e todas as diferenças que nos tornam únicos. Valorizamos a abertura, compaixão, lealdade e integridade, entendendo que é o que torna a nossa equipa verdadeiramente única.

Somos uma equipa de 8.000 profissionais para quem “Sabemos Cuidar” é um compromisso diário.

Neste momento, pretendemos recrutar um(a) Rececionista para a Clínica Lusíadas Oriente.

Perfil pretendido:

  • Habilitações académicas ao nível do 12º ano de escolaridade (obrigatório);
  • Valorizamos experiência profissional na área de receção na saúde;
  • Conhecimentos da língua inglesa a nível escrito e oral;
  • Disponibilidade para turnos rotativos (trabalho noturno, fins-de-semana e feriados).



Principais Responsabilidades:

  • Atendimento presencial ao cliente, demonstrando empatia e disponibilidade para o cliente;
  • Garantir a correta identificação, preenchimento e atualização dos dados pessoais do cliente no sistema informático;
  • Proceder à correta marcação de consultas, exames ou atos médicos;
  • Garantir a faturação dos atos realizados pelo cliente e emitir o pagamento, mediante os acordos com seguradoras;
  • Efetuar corretamente o fecho de caixa diário;
  • Efetuar a gestão de e-mails e pendentes, assegurando a sua correta priorização;
  • Demonstrar capacidade de resposta perante reclamações do cliente.



Benefícios de Ser Lusíadas:

  • Dia de aniversário oferecido;
  • Seguro de saúde para o próprio;
  • Formação contínua e mobilidade interna – Academia Lusíadas | Knowledge Center;
  • Your Baby Matters | Kit maternidade;
  • Apoios ao regresso às aulas e Natal;
  • Clube Lusíadas Vantagens | Descontos exclusivos;
  • Seguro para a família e condições especiais para agregados familiares;
  • Apoio a filhos de colaboradores com necessidades especiais – Lusíadas em Família.



No Grupo Lusíadas Saúde promovemos um ambiente inclusivo e diverso, onde valorizamos as diferenças e garantimos igualdade de oportunidades para todas as pessoas, independentemente da sua identidade, origem ou condição.

Se partilha os nossos valores e quer integrar um projeto de referência na saúde em Portugal, envie o seu CV atualizado e junte-se a uma equipa que sabe cuidar!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Expleo is a trusted partner for your innovation journey. As a global engineering, technology and consulting service provider, we are ideally positioned to help you achieve your ambitions and future-proof your business. With a smart blend of bold thinking and reliable execution, we’re able to fast-track innovation through each step of your value chain.

We are strategically positioned to build value, with a global footprint across 30 countries.

We are as global and local as you need us to be, with strong best-in-class pan-European technological centres and unique best-shoring capabilities.

We leverage a network of high value-adding affiliates in consulting and industrial excellence, and leading partners across multiple sectors to provide you with the most comprehensive services and solutions in an ever-changing environment.

Responsibilities


Perform manual data cleansing activities following defined survivorship logic and data quality rules.
Identify and resolve duplicate records across multiple datasets and systems.
Standardize names, addresses, and mandatory data fields according to established procedures.
Work with data from Excel files and local databases related to “Shadow IT” environments.
Structure, organize, and prepare legacy or fragmented data for migration activities.
Perform cross-system reconciliation between different platforms and datasets.
Compare CRM exports, SAP lists, and other data sources to identify discrepancies and data deltas.
Execute Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) accurately and consistently.
Follow global documentation and guidelines provided in English.


Essential Skills


Strong experience with manual data cleansing and data quality processes.
Experience handling data from multiple sources, including Excel files and local databases.
Ability to perform cross-system reconciliation and discrepancy analysis.
High attention to detail and accuracy when working with data.
Ability to follow SOPs and defined processes with precision.
Good English communication and comprehension skills.
Strong analytical and organizational skills.
Comfortable working with structured and unstructured datasets.


Desired Skills


Hands-on experience with Salesforce, Veeva, or SAP.
Basic SQL knowledge for data extraction and querying.
Scripting knowledge or automation experience.
Familiarity with Data Quality (DQ) tools and fuzzy-matching logic.
Experience working with AI-based data inference tools.
Previous experience working with external data providers such as IQVIA or OneKey.


What Do I Need Before I Apply


The opportunity is remote but the candidate must already be living in PT.

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
At Next Engineering, we don’t wait for the future. We build it.

We believe that innovation comes through knowledge and that every project is an opportunity to push boundaries.

We also believe that the next step is yours.


Be our NEXT Data Analyst!

  • +3 years of experience in Data Analysis, Business Intelligence, or similar roles;
  • Strong expertise in Power BI (dashboard development and data modeling);
  • Solid experience with Elasticsearch (data management and querying);
  • Proficiency in Python; basic knowledge of C# is a plus;
  • Advanced skills in Power Query;
  • Strong analytical thinking and ability to interpret complex datasets;
  • Problem-solving mindset with high attention to detail;
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills;
  • Ability to work collaboratively in cross-functional teams;
  • Manage and optimize Elasticsearch databases;
  • Build and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports;
  • Troubleshoot and resolve data integration issues;
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams on data needs;
  • Analyze data to generate actionable insights;
  • Develop Python tools to improve data processes;
  • Maintain documentation and support knowledge sharing.



Want to work hybrid in Porto?

Do you have over 3 years of experience?

Then this is what we have for you: 1100€–1400€ (base salary) + Food Allowance + Flex Benefits + other current benefits


Don’t wait either. Join Next!

Send your CV to recrutamento@nextengineering.pt

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
Com mais de 9 anos de existência a nível nacional, contamos com mais de 200 colaboradores com o nosso ADN e posicionamo-nos no mercado com 3 ofertas de serviço:

  • Strategy (Outsourcing, Team as a Service, Tech Academies);
  • R&D (Estágios, POC, Laboratório Tecnológico);
  • Nearshore.


Na ADENTIS centramo-nos nas PESSOAS e é este o nosso salário emocional:

  • Equilíbrio entre a vida profissional e pessoal;
  • Flexibilidade na rotina organizacional;
  • Benefícios de saúde para colaboradores e familiares;
  • Eventos Team Building;
  • Protocolo com mais de 600 parceiros;
  • Formação contínua através de um centro profissional de formação e certificação;
  • Acompanhamento e feedback contínuo através de um plano personalizado;
  • Progressão de carreira através de avaliações objetivas e diretas.


IMPORTANTE: Exige-se fluência em português (falado e escrito) e autorização legal para trabalhar em Portugal.


O que procuramos:


  • Licenciatura em Engenharia Informática ou área similar;
  • Experiência superior a 8 anos como Data Analyst
  • Experiência sólida com Power BI, nomeadamente na criação de dashboards e data modelling;
  • Conhecimentos de Python e Power Query (Power BI);
  • Familiaridade com ElasticSearch;
  • Bom nível de inglês (falado e escrito);
  • Disponibilidade para projeto híbrido no Porto (2x/semana).


Se queres fazer parte de uma empresa People First, junta-te a nós.


We walk with you!


REF: OPO.MPG.DAP



Na Adentis defendemos a igualdade e valorizamos a diversidade. Criamos um ambiente seguro, diversificado e onde as oportunidades são iguais para todos os colaboradores!

Não discriminamos com base em idade, etnia, orientação sexual, género, deficiência ou qualquer fator que não seja o mérito.

Todas as candidaturas com competências para o cargo são bem-vindas!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
🚀 Procuras dar um passo na tua carreira nas áreas de IT e Telecomunicações?

Então chegaste ao sítio certo – à Smart




A tecnologia, a transformação e a inovação fazem parte do nosso ADN.




Somos uma empresa de consultoria tecnológica com mais de uma década de experiência no desenvolvimento de projetos nacionais e internacionais em IT e Telecomunicações e queremos fazer parte da tua história.




🔎 O que precisas para ser um Data Analyst / BI Specialist?

Licenciatura em Engenharia Informática, Tecnologias de Informação, Data Science ou área relacionada
Entre 4 a 6 anos de experiência em análise de dados, business intelligence ou funções similares
Experiência comprovada com Power BI, incluindo criação de dashboards e modelação de dados
Familiaridade com Elasticsearch e execução de queries
Proficiência em Python; conhecimentos básicos de C# são valorizados
Bons conhecimentos de PowerQuery (Power BI)
Forte capacidade analítica e interpretação de datasets complexos
Experiência em ambientes colaborativos e equipas multidisciplinares
Elevada capacidade de resolução de problemas e atenção ao detalhe
Boas competências de comunicação e relacionamento interpessoal




💡 Quais serão as tuas funções?

Desenvolver dashboards interativos e relatórios em Power BI
Modelar, transformar e analisar dados para suporte à decisão
Trabalhar com Elasticsearch para consulta e exploração de dados
Desenvolver scripts em Python para tratamento e automação de dados
Colaborar com diferentes equipas para levantamento de requisitos de negócio
Garantir a qualidade, consistência e fiabilidade dos dados
Identificar tendências, padrões e insights relevantes
Documentar processos e soluções desenvolvidas
Apoiar na otimização de processos de análise e reporting




🌟Porquê juntar-te à nossa equipa?

Tornamos reais os projetos em que participamos.
Valorizamos a qualidade e eficiência no que fazemos.
Somos uma equipa dinâmica, íntegra e de confiança.
Oferecemos ambiente colaborativo e espaço para crescimento profissional.




📍Onde podes trabalhar?

Porto, hibrido 2x por semana




🎓Valores praticados para Júnior

Pacote Salarial: 12.880€ a 21.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis

Valores praticados para Mid

Pacote Salarial: 14.000€ a 28.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis

Valores Praticados para Sénior

Pacote Salarial: 20.000€ a 49.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis
